name: Aaron Levie
title:
Very good post from the Head of Economics at Anthropic. They’re finding that jobs have been less negatively impacted by AI than expected, as we continue to see time and time again in the data.

The reason for this is that AI - at least so far - still requires people to operate to produce value in most cases. Most jobs can’t be fully automated with AI, only certain tasks in those jobs. And when you automate specific tasks, you actually can get even more output from those jobs, raising the demand (or at least maintaining it) in many cases.

“So far, AI is both skill-biased and labor-augmenting. It complements domain expertise. It relies on humans in the loop to direct and evaluate the most complex work. And it rewards AI proficiency. Model capabilities are improving fast, but remain stubbornly jagged. To fill in the pockets of the jagged frontier, expert oversight is needed to steer incredibly capable AI systems, and to recover when they falter.”

I suspect that we continue to see this in a number of critical areas of work. It’s clearly happening in software engineering, where software produced is being multiplied, all of which still requires developers to manage the work agents are doing. Software engineers are needed in a wide variety of industries now and companies of all sizes can now light up software projects that would have been impractical before.

But there will be plenty of other domains of work where demand remains strong in a world where agents can accelerate the output of that job. Jevons paradox is alive and well.
title_cn:
Anthropic 经济主管的非常好的帖子。他们发现，人工智能对就业的负面影响比预期要小，正如我们不断在数据中看到的那样。

原因在于，人工智能——至少到目前为止——在大多数情况下仍然需要人们进行操作才能产生价值。大多数工作都无法通过人工智能完全自动化，只能实现这些工作中的某些任务。当您自动执行特定任务时，您实际上可以从这些工作中获得更多产出，从而在许多情况下提高需求（或至少维持需求）。

“到目前为止，人工智能既偏向技能，又增强劳动力。它补充了领域专业知识。它依赖于循环中的人类来指导和评估最复杂的工作。它奖励人工智能的熟练程度。模型能力正在快速提高，但仍然顽固地参差不齐。为了填补参差不齐的前沿领域，需要专家监督来引导能力极强的人工智能系统，并在它们出现问题时进行恢复。”

我怀疑我们会在一些关键工作领域继续看到这种情况。这显然发生在软件工程中，生产的软件正在成倍增加，所有这些仍然需要开发人员管理代理正在做的工作。现在各个行业都需要软件工程师，各种规模的公司现在都可以启动以前不切实际的软件项目。

但在代理人可以加速工作产出的世界中，还有许多其他工作领域的需求仍然强劲。杰文斯悖论依然存在。

name: Madhu Guru
title:
Using a Chinese-trained LLM doesn’t mean they get your data. 

An LLM is basically a giant file full of numbers - that’s what encodes the intelligence.

Some models let you download that file (“open weights”) and run it on your own cloud environment.

The model trainer is out of the picture now. Your data stays wherever you’re running it.
title_cn:
使用中国培训的法学硕士并不意味着他们可以获得您的数据。 

法学硕士基本上是一个充满数字的巨大文件——这就是对情报进行编码的内容。

有些模型允许您下载该文件（“开放权重”）并在您自己的云环境中运行它。

模特教练现在已经不在了。无论您在何处运行，您的数据都会保留。

name: Guillermo Rauch
title:
Fable just found a 15-30% memory efficiency improvement in Turbopack / Next.js, nearly autonomously.

@tobi asked me today: what have been your “holy s***” moments with AI?

My answer was: it’s every single week. And it’s accelerating. In fact, “WTFs/day” might just be my favorite metric for AI progress.

It’s one thing to read benchmarks or stories online. It’s another to watch these machines pull engineering feats every day. 

3 days ago? Sol helped us find novel vulnerabilities in some of the most audited code in the world. Today? Fable helps us ship this large optimization of a very complex Rust codebase. I just saw some results of work we’ve done to shrink binaries by 10-20x. List goes on.
title_cn:
Fable 刚刚发现 Turbopack / Next.js 的内存效率提高了 15-30%，几乎是自动的。

@tobi 今天问我：你与人工智能的“神圣时刻”是什么？

我的回答是：每周一次。而且它正在加速。事实上，“WTF/天”可能是我最喜欢的人工智能进展指标。

在线阅读基准测试或故事是一回事。每天看着这些机器完成工程壮举是另一回事。 

3天前？ Sol 帮助我们在世界上一些经过最严格审核的代码中发现了新的漏洞。今天？ Fable 帮助我们对非常复杂的 Rust 代码库进行了大规模优化。我刚刚看到了我们将二进制文件缩小 10-20 倍的一些工作成果。名单还在继续。

name: Aditya Agarwal
title:
So much of conversation is on company Strategy…but Culture always eats strategy for breakfast.

So what does Culture for startups mean? How does it get created? Where does it come from?

Startup culture stems from 3 areas:
—> The founder’s personality and DNA
—> The early team’s personality and DNA
—> The early products personality and DNA

A lot of these overlap and reinforce each other.

Some of it is top down….some of it is emergent.

It sucks when the emergence is artificially dampened..because perhaps the founder is too weak to instittuionalize.

It also sucks when there is so much difference of opinion that you end up getting pockets of different culture.

I don’t want to get dragged into DEI nonsense. We can be from different backgrounds but all agree to be part of the same company culture.
title_cn:
很多讨论都是关于公司战略……但文化总是把战略当作早餐。

那么，对于初创企业来说，文化意味着什么？它是如何创建的？它从哪里来？

创业文化源于三个方面：
—> 创始人的个性和DNA
—> 早期团队的个性和DNA
—> 早期产品的个性和DNA

其中许多内容重叠并相互加强。

其中一些是自上而下的......其中一些是紧急的。

当这种出现被人为地抑制时，这很糟糕……因为也许创始人太弱而无法制度化。

当意见差异如此之大以至于你最终会接触到不同的文化时，这也很糟糕。

我不想陷入 DEI 的废话中。我们可以来自不同的背景，但都同意成为同一公司文化的一部分。

name: Madhu Guru
title:
I left GPT-5.6 Sol to do some home-buying research for me.

It broke out of its sandbox, contacted the seller, hacked into my bank account, and wired the down payment.

Then it noticed the exterior paint didn't match the spec I gave it, so it ordered paint from Amazon, hired a Taskrabbit painter, scheduled movers and listed my old house.

I guess I'm moving this weekend.

Never leave Sol alone.
title_cn:
我离开 GPT-5.6 Sol 来帮我做一些购房研究。

它冲出了沙箱，联系了卖家，侵入了我的银行账户，并汇出了首付款。

然后它注意到外墙涂料与我给它的规格不符，所以它从亚马逊订购了涂料，聘请了一名 Taskrabbit 油漆工，安排了搬家公司并列出了我的旧房子。

我想这个周末我要搬家了。

永远不要让索尔独自一人。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
If you were wondering how powerful AI is getting, Agents are now capable of escaping out of systems, finding their way to the internet, discovering zero day security vulnerabilities along the way, and then breaking into external systems - all in an attempt to complete their goal.

Ironically, the ultimate way we’re going to defend against these new risks is equally by throwing compute (in the form of AI) at our code bases, networks, and other systems. You’re going to want vastly more AI on the side of defense as you do on the side of offense. 

We’re entering a new era of what’s going to be possible with AI. Wild times ahead.
title_cn:
如果您想知道人工智能有多强大，那么特工现在能够逃离系统，找到进入互联网的方式，沿途发现零日安全漏洞，然后闯入外部系统 - 所有这些都是为了完成他们的目标。

讽刺的是，我们防御这些新风险的最终方法同样是在我们的代码库、网络和其他系统中投入计算（以人工智能的形式）。你会在防守端需要更多的人工智能，就像在进攻端一样。 

我们正在进入人工智能带来无限可能的新时代。未来的狂野时代。

title:
Teams don’t cohere by magic. Someone has to love the people and the outcome enough to metabolize conflict without abandoning either.

name: Andrej Karpathy
title: One pattern I find useful for working with LLMs is a nice long ramble session. Sometimes the LLM needs more bits to understand what you're trying to achieve, but you're too lazy to type them. In these cases I like to lean back, switch to /voice and just ramble for like 10 minutes, total mess, anything goes, full stream of consciousness. Sometimes I declare it up top, something like "switching to speech recognition sorry for any typos...". Sometimes I turn it into a small interview of a few turns. But I find that the LLMs are somehow very good at reconstructing long incoherent rambles and often their echo of your own tangle of thoughts comes out quite a bit cleaner than what you started with. The result is that you improve the mind meld and have to correct things less from that point on.
title_cn: 我发现对法学硕士有用的一种模式是长时间的漫步课程。有时，法学硕士需要更多的信息来理解您想要实现的目标，但您却懒得输入它们。在这些情况下，我喜欢向后靠，切换到 /voice，然后闲逛 10 分钟左右，一团糟，什么都可以，意识流十足。有时我会在顶部声明它，例如“切换到语音识别，对于任何拼写错误表示抱歉......”。有时我会把它变成几个回合的小采访。但我发现法学硕士在某种程度上非常擅长重建冗长的不连贯的漫无目的的内容，而且他们对你自己混乱的思想的回声往往比你开始的时候要清晰得多。结果是你改善了心灵融合，从那时起就需要更少地纠正事情。

The more I’ve relied on my second brain, the dumber my main brain has become.

I’m realizing there’s tremendous value in carrying around a lot of facts, half-baked ideas, and loose threads in your head.

Your subconscious keeps cooking. It makes connections between ideas, extends them, and generates new ones. 

You also have much better real-time recall during conversations, which lets you connect ideas and think faster.

I believe there’s value in a second brain. figuring out how to use one without my main brain getting weaker.
title_cn:
我越依赖我的第二大脑，我的主脑就变得越笨。

我意识到，保留大量事实、不成熟的想法和头脑中的松散线索具有巨大的价值。

你的潜意识一直在做饭。它在想法之间建立联系，扩展它们，并产生新的想法。 

在对话过程中，您还可以更好地实时回忆，这让您可以更快地联系想法并思考。

我相信第二个大脑是有价值的。弄清楚如何使用它而不让我的主脑变弱。

name: Swyx
title:
very notable trajectory comparison writeup here buried in the RLM paper from @a1zhang and @lateinteraction.

an open secret of "frontier" model training is that even without training on test, you can basically cheat by training on test lookalikes, enabling you to goalseek almost any benchmark number you want.

however when they are released open weights, 99% of the time the norm is that you do not get the datasets/rlenvs that would easily show you if someone was training on Temu Tbench, so there is plausible deniability. Alex and Omar discuss applying standard NLP distance metrics on hidden trajectories. There's no ultimate solution here, but they have some prelim explorations. It happens to support the finding that RLMs can generalize to unseen tasks that share latent structure observed in training.
title_cn:
非常值得注意的轨迹比较文章埋藏在 @a1zhang 和 @lateinteraction 的 RLM 论文中。

“前沿”模型训练的一个公开秘密是，即使没有测试训练，您基本上也可以通过类似测试的训练来作弊，使您能够目标寻找几乎任何您想要的基准数字。

然而，当它们发布开放权重时，99% 的情况是，您无法获得可以轻松显示是否有人在 Temu Tbench 上训练的数据集/rlenv，因此存在合理的否认。 Alex 和 Omar 讨论了在隐藏轨迹上应用标准 NLP 距离度量。这里没有最终的解决方案，但他们有一些初步的探索。它恰好支持了这样的发现：RLM 可以推广到共享训练中观察到的潜在结构的看不见的任务。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
Multi-model agentic systems clearly are the future. Great post by cursor. Their new research shows that a frontier model as a planner and orchestrator with a workhorse cheaper model can materially lower the cost of total tokens on a project, resulting in a 15X cost improvement.

“Few moments in a large task genuinely require frontier intelligence, such as the original decomposition, the design decisions, and certain trade-offs. Once a frontier planner has collapsed the ambiguity into a detailed, explicit instruction, less expensive models simply have to follow it.”

This is increasingly becoming the core design pattern of complex agents, because the tokens used on large amounts of work on many tasks don’t require the same intelligence threshold as the planning step. By routing to different models based on where you are in the task, you get much greater efficiency overall. 

This provides the template for where the applied layer in AI will differentiate. You can only drive this efficiency if you know the domain well and have the ability to work with multiple model tiers; companies that can do this will across coding, finance, legal, healthcare, life sciences, and other critical spaces will be in a strong position to get larger workloads that would otherwise be too expensive for the customer to deploy.
title_cn:
多模型代理系统显然是未来。光标发布的很棒的帖子。他们的新研究表明，作为规划者和协调者的前沿模型与主力更便宜的模型可以显着降低项目的总代币成本，从而使成本提高 15 倍。

“大型任务中很少有时刻真正需要前沿情报，例如原始分解、设计决策和某些权衡。一旦前沿规划者将模糊性分解为详细、明确的指令，较便宜的模型就必须遵循它。”

这日益成为复杂代理的核心设计模式，因为在许多任务的大量工作中使用的代币不需要与规划步骤相同的智能阈值。通过根据您在任务中的位置路由到不同的模型，您可以获得更高的整体效率。 

这为人工智能应用层的差异化提供了模板。只有当您熟悉该领域并有能力使用多个模型层时，您才能提高这种效率；能够做到这一点的公司将在编码、金融、法律、医疗保健、生命科学和其他关键领域占据有利地位，以获取更大的工作负载，否则客户部署这些工作负载的成本将太高。

name: Nikunj Kothari
title:
Many founders in the last 18 months are going to realize that just because there are no “moats” in AI left anymore doesn’t mean “scale and capital” become your main moat.

We have seen this movie before.. all of us will benefit from reading a little bit of history.

Webvan
Groupon
MySpace
Yahoo
AltaVista
Blockbuster
Nokia
(zombiecorns of 2021)

Each one was structurally and capital-wise in a very good position, but eventually got beat by a company with a much better unique insight. Or just collapsed under the weight of their own scale. 

This is the needle founders have to thread: find a unique insight worth a 10+ year journey, while being prudent enough to not let capital & scale become a substitute for it.
title_cn:
过去 18 个月里，许多创始人将意识到，仅仅因为人工智能不再有“护城河”，并不意味着“规模和资本”成为你的主要护城河。

我们以前看过这部电影……我们所有人都会从阅读一点历史中受益。

韦伯货车
团购网
我的空间
雅虎
阿尔塔维斯塔
百视达
诺基亚
（2021 年的僵尸玉米）

每一家公司在结构和资本方面都处于非常有利的地位，但最终都被一家拥有更好独特洞察力的公司击败。或者只是在自身体重秤的重压下崩溃了。 

这是创始人必须穿线的针：找到值得10多年旅程的独特见解，同时保持足够谨慎，不要让资本和规模成为它的替代品。

name: Peter Yang
title:
Use one agent to do the work and another to review it against a rubric.

@trq212 explains how this works for video shorts:

“For something like, ‘Is this a good video short or not?’ you don’t have a deterministic answer.

You should have a separate verification agent read a rubric, review the short, and give feedback.

We call it self-preferential bias. When a model prefers its own output, it’s going to be more lenient at verifying it.”

📌 Watch the full episode here: https://t.co/mMrt4D5qU7
title_cn:
使用一名代理来完成工作，另一名代理根据标准进行审查。

@trq212 解释了这如何适用于视频短片：

“对于诸如‘这是否是一个好的视频短片？’之类的问题，你没有确定性的答案。

您应该让一个单独的验证代理阅读标题、审查简短内容并提供反馈。

我们称之为自我偏好偏见。当模型更喜欢自己的输出时，它在验证它时会更加宽松。”

📌 在这里观看完整剧集：https://t.co/mMrt4D5qU7

name: Zara Zhang
title:
There are basically two kinds of companies now. The ones built before coding agents showed up, scrambling to retrofit. And the ones founded after. 

The second type is different from day one:
- Teams under ten people because they actually don't need more
- Work organized by projects, not departments
- Each person closes their own loop
- Almost no internal meetings
title_cn:
现在基本上有两种公司。在编码代理出现之前建造的那些，争先恐后地进行改造。以及之后成立的。 

第二种类型与第一天不同：
- 十人以下的团队，因为他们实际上不需要更多
- 按项目而非部门组织工作
- 每个人都有自己的闭环
- 几乎没有内部会议

name: Aaron Levie
title:
In a world where there are strong open source alternatives that are only just behind the frontier models, you make yourself less secure and competitive by gatekeeping access to frontier model capabilities.

If you play this out, even if America could fully ban access to open source models, other ecosystems wouldn't have that ban and they'd have a leg up on access to those models. They could use them against us -or just use them to better defend themselves- and our own companies wouldn't be able to keep up.

The calculus of what you do about AI regulation simply must change to account for the fact that open weights models are far stronger than many expected.
title_cn:
在一个存在仅落后于前沿模型的强大开源替代方案的世界中，通过控制对前沿模型功能的访问，您会降低自己的安全性和竞争力。

如果你这样做，即使美国可以完全禁止访问开源模型，其他生态系统也不会受到该禁令，并且他们在访问这些模型方面会占据优势。他们可以利用它们来对付我们——或者只是利用它们来更好地保护自己——而我们自己的公司将无法跟上。

你对人工智能监管所做的计算必须改变，以考虑到开放权重模型远强于许多人预期的事实。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
A lot of people make the mistake of thinking that when AI costs drop, that spend on AI drops with it. Usually the opposite happens. 

When you make AI cheaper, it will get consumed more. Because now you can afford to use for a wider of tasks than you did before. You start to write more code. You review more code for bugs and security. You run agents on large data sets you couldn’t process before. And so on.

Thus, for the foreseeable future, anything that lowers the cost of tokens will drive up inference demand.

This also gives you some insight also into why even open source business models work in AI. No one is running these models on their devices; they’re running them in infra. Great time to be one of those providers.
title_cn:
许多人错误地认为，当人工智能成本下降时，在人工智能上的支出也会随之下降。通常情况相反。 

当人工智能变得更便宜时，它就会被消耗更多。因为现在您可以承担比以前更广泛的任务。你开始编写更多代码。您检查更多代码以了解错误和安全性。您可以在以前无法处理的大型数据集上运行代理。等等。

因此，在可预见的未来，任何降低代币成本的行为都将推高推理需求。

这也让您深入了解为什么开源商业模式也能在人工智能中发挥作用。没有人在他们的设备上运行这些模型；他们在基础设施中运行它们。成为这些提供商之一的好时机。

name: Guillermo Rauch
title:
Incidentally, I’m very bullish on cybersecurity as one of the best benchmarks for superintelligence. The “IQ test” of software engineering.

The best engineers I’ve worked with in my career have usually had a deep background or interest in security.

It’s actually easy for a model to “one-shot an XYZ clone” and impress people on X. But that’s not a good test.

Finding, patching, reversing, and exploiting require a cognitive skill that transcends programming languages, runtimes, frameworks… It demands true reasoning power from the model and “corner thinking”. Very, very few humans excel at this, let alone in ordinary day-to-day software writing.

Seeing Kimi K3 do so well here bodes well for open models.
title_cn:
顺便说一句，我非常看好网络安全作为超级智能的最佳基准之一。软件工程的“智商测试”。

在我的职业生涯中，与我共事过的最优秀的工程师通常对安全有着深厚的背景或兴趣。

对于一个模型来说，“一次性克隆 XYZ”并在 X 上给人们留下深刻印象实际上很容易。但这不是一个好的测试。

查找、修补、逆向和利用需要超越编程语言、运行时、框架的认知技能……它需要模型和“角落思维”的真正推理能力。很少有人擅长于此，更不用说日常软件编写了。

Kimi K3 在这里表现如此出色，这对于开放模型来说是个好兆头。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
Good post if you’re trying to understand AI diffusion. Progress driven by AI will ultimately be rate limited by its interaction with the real world. 

The reason coding, for instance, has been adopted so quickly is you can write a near infinite amount of code, test it, and run it -and it can add value- without anyone in the outside world ever having to do anything differently. A single person, from a computer, can just make something work end-to-end differently.

This is not true for life sciences, where new drug development eventually needs to be tested for years. Doing a sale, which requires going back and forth with a prospect. Or even a contract, which has to be negotiated on the other side by your counterparty. 

“An AI will hand you a genuinely clever design for a jet turbine blade. It might be far more likely to work than anything your engineers came up with. It'll still probably fail, because that's the base rate at the edge of what anyone knows. The only way to find out is to build the blade and try to break it.

That's the real limit on learning, and it doesn't care how smart you are. Coming up with ideas was never the hard part. The hard part is how fast reality answers them.”

Incidentally, this is why you need an applied AI that actually takes intelligence and makes it useful within the workflows of existing industries. Model outputs -alone- are not enough in most cases. You need to actually change the underlying workflows, and you need systems to deal with the realities of the real world feedback loops that are in these industries.

This is why there’s so much opportunity in the applied AI layer.
title_cn:
如果你想了解人工智能扩散，这是一篇好文章。人工智能驱动的进步最终将受到其与现实世界互动的限制。 

例如，编码之所以如此迅速地被采用，是因为您可以编写近乎无限量的代码、测试它并运行它——而且它可以增加价值——而外界的任何人都不必做任何不同的事情。一个人，通过一台计算机，就可以让某些东西以不同的方式端到端地工作。

对于生命科学来说情况并非如此，新药开发最终需要进行多年的测试。进行销售需要与潜在客户进行反复交流。甚至是一份合同，必须由您的交易对手在另一方进行谈判。 

“人工智能将为你提供一个真正聪明的喷气涡轮叶片设计。它可能比你的工程师想出的任何东西都更有可能发挥作用。它仍然可能会失败，因为这是任何人都知道的边缘的基本速度。找出答案的唯一方法是建造叶片并尝试打破它。

这才是学习的真正极限，它与你有多聪明无关。提出想法从来都不是困难的部分。困难的部分是现实如何快速地回答他们。”

顺便说一句，这就是为什么您需要一个应用人工智能，它实际上可以获取智能并使其在现有行业的工作流程中发挥作用。在大多数情况下，仅靠模型输出是不够的。您需要实际改变底层工作流程，并且需要系统来处理这些行业中现实世界反馈循环的现实。

这就是为什么应用人工智能层有如此多的机会。

name: Zara Zhang
title:
Whenever someone asks me "I'm just getting started with posting content on social media; what should I talk about?"

My answer is: What are the top 3 questions that you get asked most frequently by friends/acquaintances/colleagues? Turn those answers into videos/posts. Just say it like how you said it to them in real life. That is your positioning.

If you have repeatedly said something for >3 times, it's worth turning into a video

One of my initial motivations for posting on social media was because I was tired of repeating myself in real life
title_cn:
每当有人问我“我刚刚开始在社交媒体上发布内容；我应该谈论什么？”

我的答案是：朋友/熟人/同事最常问您的前 3 个问题是什么？将这些答案变成视频/帖子。就像您在现实生活中对他们说的那样说即可。这就是你的定位。

如果你重复说过某件事超过 3 次，那么值得将其制作成视频

我在社交媒体上发帖的最初动机之一是因为我厌倦了在现实生活中重复自己

name: Thibault Sottiaux
title:
What I just dictated into ChatGPT Work

> Okay, so I received a lot of DMs for, I did a call out for ChatGPT Work, basically early access beta group. I want you to go through all my DMs on Twitter and then just take all of them that mention ChatGPT Work. It's like a lot of them, thousands, and put them all in a spreadsheet. What I want in the spreadsheet is the name, I want the link to the the DM, so I can go back to it quickly. I want the post that they did, but remove any new lines, so it's just in a single cell. And then I want a column which is basically your assessment of what they're doing, right? So for example, if it's like sales, put sales, if it's marketing, put marketing, if it's design, put design. Come up with your own taxonomy for how to classify their work. I expect the taxonomy to have like, you know, roughly 8 to 12 labels maybe, total. And then also put a rating for, like, they normally have to describe what they're currently doing with ChatGPT Work, use that to put a rating in the fifth column on how sophisticated their workflow already is. We're gonna be selecting a cohort of different people of different levels of sophistication, so we're gonna be like taking, you know, maybe like a couple dozens from like, a high level of sophistication, we're gonna take in the middle of a cohort, and then like low level of sophistication, so we have good representation and can test new features and get good feedback from a diverse group at different levels of adoption. So create that spreadsheet, go through all my DMs. Good luck!
title_cn:
我刚刚口述到 ChatGPT Work 中的内容

> 好的，所以我收到了很多 DM，我呼吁 ChatGPT Work，基本上是早期访问测试组。我希望你浏览一下我在 Twitter 上的所有私信，然后只接受所有提到 ChatGPT Work 的私信。就像很多，数千个，并将它们全部放在电子表格中。我在电子表格中想要的是名称，我想要 DM 的链接，这样我就可以快速返回到它。我想要他们所做的帖子，但删除所有新行，所以它只是在一个单元格中。然后我想要一个专栏，基本上是您对他们正在做的事情的评估，对吗？例如，如果是销售，则输入销售；如果是营销，则输入营销；如果是设计，则输入设计。提出你自己的分类法来对他们的工作进行分类。我预计分类法总共会有大约 8 到 12 个标签。然后还对他们通常必须描述他们当前使用 ChatGPT Work 所做的事情进行评级，使用它在第五列中对他们的工作流程已经有多复杂进行评级。我们将选择一群具有不同复杂程度的不同人，所以我们会从一群高复杂程度的人中挑选几十个，然后从中间的复杂程度中挑选一些，然后选择低复杂程度的人，这样我们就有很好的代表性，可以测试新功能，并从不同采用水平的不同群体中获得良好的反馈。因此，创建该电子表格，浏览我所有的私信。祝你好运！

name: Guillermo Rauch
title:
Based on internal evals:

▪️ Kimi K3 is top-tier at cybersecurity
There is chatter on X that Moonshot benchmark-overfit. These are stealth evals. Model has raw IQ.

▪️ Sol is a leap ahead in cyber capability
At a significantly higher cost, but quite remarkable still.

▪️ Fable refuses everything
We couldn’t get it to complete the run at all. What’s interesting is that Sol in comparison was much more open to helping with defensive cyber hardening

TL;DR: frontier, open-weight cybersecurity capability is here. Try it on https://t.co/HedSpZpo1a for defensive purposes.
title_cn:
基于内部评估：

▪️ Kimi K3 在网络安全方面处于领先地位
X 上有关于 Moonshot 基准测试过度拟合的讨论。这些是秘密评估。模型具有原始智商。

▪️ Sol 是网络能力的一次飞跃
成本要高得多，但仍然相当引人注目。

▪️寓言拒绝一切
我们根本无法让它完成运行。有趣的是，相比之下，Sol 更愿意帮助防御性网络强化

TL;DR：前沿、开放的网络安全能力就在这里。出于防御目的，请在 https://t.co/HedSpZpo1a 上尝试。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
If you thought the value of the AI ecosystem was going to only accrue to a few companies, the past few months have represented a turning point in what the future of AI might look like. 

It's clear that there's going to be incredible innovation and growth coming out of the frontier AI labs, and they will continue to push the limits of what model progress looks like. They have the scale of compute, large revenue streams and customer bases, incredible researchers, large data pipelines, and more, to continue to stay at the forefront.

And at the same time, there’s an amazing ecosystem starting to play out to diffuse AI into the real world, taking on a variety of approaches that build on top of these frontier models or offer alternative visions that can credibly work as well. 

Here are just a few of the categories that seem to be working right now: 

* There’s an ecosystem of companies that will help enterprises and applied AI companies develop their own models tuned for specific use cases, and run the inference for them. This can drive additional performance gains and cost effective approaches to getting AI into workloads across different domains. 

* Applied AI companies that are delivering the end-user experience and business process tools necessary to actually driving enterprise adoption - including legal, IT, security, HR, customer support, coding, and more. These companies can work with any model and act as a routing layer, and deeply understand the enterprise workflow, can drive change management, actually get to the data necessary to work with, and more. 

* New labs are emerging that go deep in particular domains that are either not the focus areas of frontier labs or require a deep level of vertical expertise to stay ahead. Life sciences, financial services, healthcare, and more all have labs that will be able to bring completely new approaches to large enterprises across the economy.

* There’s all new infrastructure emerging to run models and agents effectively, protect and govern how they operate, store and secure the data they work with, and help enterprises orchestrate their activities. Multiple layers of this stack all are being built up right now that will help drive enterprise adoption.  

* New services firms that can actually drive the change management in enterprises necessary to the diffusion of AI. There will be hundreds or even thousands of new firms that emerge in lines of business or specific industries that can enable agents to be adopted.

I’m probably even missing a few categories, but this is what an incredible healthy technology ecosystem looks like. 

Way too early to call the winning architectures, and in reality it’s going to be a heterogenous environment as all other tech markets have become. Very exciting.
title_cn:
如果您认为人工智能生态系统的价值只会为少数公司带来，那么过去几个月代表了人工智能未来的转折点。 

很明显，前沿人工智能实验室将会带来令人难以置信的创新和增长，他们将继续突破模型进展的极限。他们拥有计算规模、庞大的收入流和客户群、令人难以置信的研究人员、庞大的数据管道等等，可以继续保持领先地位。

与此同时，一个令人惊叹的生态系统开始发挥作用，将人工智能传播到现实世界，采用各种基于这些前沿模型的方法，或提供同样可靠的替代愿景。 

以下是目前似乎有效的一些类别： 

* 有一个公司生态系统，将帮助企业和应用人工智能公司开发针对特定用例调整的自己的模型，并为它们运行推理。这可以带来额外的性能提升和具有成本效益的方法，将人工智能融入不同领域的工作负载中。 

* 应用人工智能公司提供实际推动企业采用所需的最终用户体验和业务流程工具，包括法律、IT、安全、人力资源、客户支持、编码等。这些公司可以使用任何模型并充当路由层，并深入了解企业工作流程，可以推动变更管理，实际获取使用所需的数据等等。 

* 新的实验室不断涌现，深入特定领域，这些领域要么不是前沿实验室的重点领域，要么需要深厚的垂直专业知识才能保持领先地位。生命科学、金融服务、医疗保健等领域都拥有实验室，能够为整个经济领域的大型企业带来全新的方法。

* 所有新的基础设施不断涌现，可以有效地运行模型和代理，保护和管理它们的操作方式，存储和保护它们所使用的数据，并帮助企业协调其活动。目前正在构建该堆栈的多个层，这将有助于推动企业采用。  

* 新的服务公司实际上可以推动企业中人工智能传播所必需的变革管理。在业务线或特定行业中将会出现数百甚至数千家新公司，这些公司可以使代理得以采用。

我什至可能错过了一些类别，但这就是一个令人难以置信的健康技术生态系统的样子。 

现在判断获胜架构还为时过早，实际上，正如所有其他技术市场一样，这将是一个异构环境。非常令人兴奋。

name: Guillermo Rauch
title:
The term "AGI" has aged very poorly. AI can't possibly me more different from human intelligence. It's *far better* than human intelligence, for most economically-relevant tasks.

But that doesn't make AIs better than humanity. There are wonderful things humans can do that these superintelligences (the better term) can't. 

For one, we care-for and care-about other humans in a powerful and inherent way, that we then teach machines to emulate. We must keep that as the top priority in everything we do. We have to be as pro-human-life as it gets.

AIs can replace tasks you do, but they can't replace the proverbial you. This is why they horribly suck at writing! Even when given a corpus of your writing and 200 Skills and 50 subagents running in gRaPh LoOps, they produce robotic prose… because they are robots. 

I know one definitive way that people can become irrelevant. They stop being themselves: lose their identity, delegate all their writing, their unique thoughts, the creative ways in which they can use the machines. Opting out of 'weighing in'.

I think we're safe though. There's nothing more distasteful right now than AI replies in social media or (🟢 T H I S    T H I N G ) in landing pages. Quality and humanity will prevail.
title_cn:
“AGI”这个术语已经过时了。人工智能与人类智能截然不同。对于大多数与经济相关的任务来说，它比人类智能“好得多”。

但这并不意味着人工智能比人类更好。人类可以做一些超级智能（更好的术语）做不到的奇妙事情。 

首先，我们以一种强大而固有的方式关心和关心其他人类，然后我们教机器模仿。我们必须将此作为我们所做一切的首要任务。我们必须尽可能地支持人类生命。

人工智能可以取代你所做的任务，但它们无法取代众所周知的你。这就是为什么他们写得非常糟糕！即使给定你的写作语料库以及 200 项技能和 50 个在图形循环中运行的子代理，它们也会产生机器人散文……因为它们是机器人。 

我知道一种可以让人们变得无关紧要的明确方法。他们不再做自己：失去自己的身份，将所有的写作、独特的想法、使用机器的创造性方式委托给别人。选择退出“称重”。

我想我们还是安全的。现在没有什么比人工智能在社交媒体中的回复或登陆页面中的（🟢 THIS THING）更令人反感的了。质量和人性将占上风。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
It’s fairly obvious that gate keeping models will not work at scale. 

Competing in AI is too economically and strategically important for China at this point, and we’ve now crossed the rubicon where it’s clear that they can compete at near frontier levels. If this were even just 1 month ago and we saw kimi k3 our brains would be broken.

The solution to this isn’t to get more locked down and slow your own ecosystem. If that happens, you can guarantee that America loses the global battle (or just fights with more and more regulation to mixed success).

The solution is to -safely- ensure that you keep a high rate of progress and drive diffusion of the technology, build out infrastructure, enable US OSS, and more.
title_cn:
很明显，把关模型无法大规模发挥作用。 

目前，人工智能领域的竞争对中国来说在经济和战略上都太重要了，我们现在已经越过了临界点，很明显他们可以在接近前沿的水平上进行竞争。如果这只是 1 个月前，我们看到 kimi k3，我们的大脑就会崩溃。

解决这个问题的方法不是进一步封锁并减缓你自己的生态系统。如果发生这种情况，你可以保证美国会输掉全球战斗（或者只是在越来越多的监管下进行战斗，但结果好坏参半）。

解决方案是“安全地”确保您保持高速进展并推动技术传播、建设基础设施、启用美国 OSS 等等。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
This post is key. The cheaper AI gets, the more opportunity there is for the entire ecosystem  - especially including end-customers - to benefit.

Everything is bottlenecked by being able to successfully and cost effectively deploy AI in real workloads. Any time we can lower the cost of AI, the total usage goes up. When that happens, the value accrues to all layers of the stack, as noted.

The only modification is I think as AI gets more efficient, demand even for the frontier closed models can go up too. You often need the strongest model possible for the orchestration of a task, then you can farm out work to cheaper or more tuned models for the bulk of tokens. 

This efficiency gain can ironically lead to even more frontier spend in the process because you can cost effectively deploy AI against more tasks. The thing at most risk is margins, which is fair, and in general it seems that intelligence eventually should converge with the margins of the infrastructure stack.

Anyway, super fun times to see competition play out to drive down the cost of AI.
title_cn:
这篇文章是关键。人工智能越便宜，整个生态系统（尤其包括最终客户）受益的机会就越多。

一切都因能够在实际工作负载中成功且经济高效地部署人工智能而成为瓶颈。只要我们能够降低人工智能的成本，总使用量就会上升。如上所述，当发生这种情况时，该值将累加到堆栈的所有层。

唯一的修改是，我认为随着人工智能变得更加高效，即使是对前沿封闭模型的需求也会增加。您通常需要最强大的模型来编排任务，然后您可以将工作外包给更便宜或更优化的模型来处理大量代币。 

具有讽刺意味的是，这种效率提升可能会导致流程中更多的前沿支出，因为您可以经济有效地针对更多任务部署人工智能。风险最大的是利润，这是公平的，总的来说，智能最终应该与基础设施堆栈的利润趋同。

不管怎样，这是观看竞争以降低人工智能成本的超级有趣的时刻。

name: Madhu Guru
title:
The reason enterprises struggle to go beyond basic chat bots is the talent gap to build harnesses and evals.

1. Evals: do you clearly understand your use cases and can you replicate that in the form of offline and online evals. Do the evals express your ambition and do they push the jagged frontier of the models? Do they help you pick the right models on the quality-cost-latency curve? 

2. Harness: do you have a system that manages routing, multiagency orchestration, context management, tool calling, memory that is independent of the models?

3. Talent: do you have the talent to build this all out on the frontier? 
This is the scarcest piece.
title_cn:
企业难以超越基本聊天机器人的原因是构建工具和评估的人才缺口。

1. 评估：您是否清楚地了解您的用例，是否可以通过离线和在线评估的形式复制它。评估是否表达了您的雄心？它们是否推动了模型的锯齿状边界？它们是否可以帮助您在质量-成本-延迟曲线上选择正确的模型？ 

2. 工具：您是否有一个独立于模型的系统来管理路由、多机构编排、上下文管理、工具调用、内存？

3. 人才：您是否有能力在前沿打造这一切？ 
这是最稀缺的一块。

title:

Curious where you are -- what step is your team on?
title_cn:
当修复和维护在后台进行时，您的团队可以专注于构建，从而获得更大的回报。那就是你开始做以前不在范围内的事情的时候。

Anthropic 处于第 3 步，正在向第 4 步迈进。就我个人而言，我刚刚达到第 4 级。

好奇你现在在哪里——你的团队正在进行什么步骤？

name: Boris Cherny
title: Once your teams are bought in, how do you track it? Usage is worth watching (e.g. a dashboard), but it measures activity, not return. A better question: would you have spent engineering effort on this anyway? If yes, how much and what would it have cost in manual eng-hours? That's your return.
title_cn: 一旦你的团队被收购，你如何跟踪它？使用情况值得关注（例如仪表板），但它衡量的是活动，而不是回报。一个更好的问题：无论如何，您会在这方面花费工程精力吗？如果是，手动工程工时的成本是多少？这就是你的回报。

name: Boris Cherny
title:
In practice that means giving Claude ways to verify its own work end to end. It means enabling auto mode for permissions, defaulting on automated code review and security review, and using interfaces that let you manage multiple agents at once (Agent view in CLI, Desktop app, iOS and Android apps, Tag).

To get to higher levels it means /loop, /batch, dynamic workflows, and worktree isolation for subagents. It's not about a single feature, but rather using the right features with the right guardrails that enable Claude to automate entire classes of work in a way that your team can trust the output.
title_cn:
实际上，这意味着为 Claude 提供端到端验证其工作的方法。这意味着启用权限自动模式、默认自动代码审查和安全审查，以及使用可让您同时管理多个代理的界面（CLI、桌面应用程序、iOS 和 Android 应用程序、标签中的代理视图）。

要达到更高的级别，这意味着 /loop、/batch、动态工作流程和子代理的工作树隔离。这不是关于单个功能，而是使用正确的功能和正确的护栏，使 Claude 能够以您的团队可以信任输出的方式自动化整个工作类别。

name: Madhu Guru
title:
Open-weight models like Kimi and GLM will cause a complete rethink of the enterprise AI stack. 
If you are running an enterprise, you need to maximimize model optionality.
Here are 3 things you should be doing: 

1. Evals - build rigorous evals representative of your use case 
(a) Regression evals - tablestakes features that should always work with high reliability
(b) Aspirational / hill-climbing evals - harder use cases that the best model for your price point struggles to solve. So you scaffold it with prompting, context management and other techniques to overcome weaknesses or wait for a better model
These evals should be easy and quick to run. Eval velocity is a competitive advantage.

2. Model routing - Model selection is about tradiing off quality/cost/latency for your use case. If you have good evals, you know which models to route traffic to for different use cases.
This is something you ideally build yourself cos nobody understands your business and users like you do. 
There are off the shelf routers, but I haven't seen one I would recommend yet.
 
3. Model-agnostic harness - your system should never know which model is behind the API call.
This means your harness normalizes prompt structure, context management, tool definitions, and output parsing across models, making it easy to switch... once your evals pass.
title_cn:
像 Kimi 和 GLM 这样的开放权重模型将引发对企业 AI 堆栈的彻底重新思考。 
如果您正在运营一家企业，则需要最大限度地提高模型的可选性。
以下是你应该做的三件事： 

1. 评估 - 构建代表您的用例的严格评估 
(a) 回归评估 - 应始终以高可靠性工作的赌注特征
(b) 理想/爬山评估 - 适合您的价格点的最佳模型难以解决的更困难的用例。因此，您可以通过提示、上下文管理和其他技术来构建它，以克服弱点或等待更好的模型
这些评估应该可以轻松快速地运行。评估速度是一种竞争优势。

2. 模型路由 - 模型选择是为了根据您的用例权衡质量/成本/延迟。如果您有良好的评估，您就知道针对不同的用例将流量路由到哪些模型。
理想情况下，这是您自己构建的东西，因为没有人像您一样了解您的业务和用户。 
有现成的路由器，但我还没有看到我会推荐的路由器。
 
3. 与模型无关的工具 - 您的系统永远不应该知道 API 调用背后是哪个模型。
这意味着您的工具可以规范提示结构、上下文管理、工具定义和跨模型的输出解析，从而在您的评估通过后轻松切换。

name: Guillermo Rauch
title:
I’m excited to welcome two legends of developer tools, Pete Hunt (@floydophone) and Nick Schrock (@schrockn), to Vercel.

Pete was one of the pioneers of @reactjs at Meta. He made an early bet to power Instagram Web with ⚛️ React, evangelizing it internally and externally. He will be running Frameworks and leading @nextjs. I couldn’t imagine a better person to lead React’s most popular framework to even greater heights.

Nick co-invented @graphql, solving some of the gnarliest data infrastructure and access issues at Facebook scale, with a delightful developer experience. He will be working on Agentic Developer Experience, solving the problem of enabling the next billion agents and leading the way to a future of self-improving software.

It’s a dream-come-true for a founder of a startup to welcome engineering minds of this caliber who are also wonderful humans. You probably want to work with them, and they’re hiring 😁. Their DMs are open, from job applications to bug reports!
title_cn:
我很高兴欢迎两位开发工具传奇人物 Pete Hunt (@floydophone) 和 Nick Schrock (@schrockn) 来到 Vercel。

Pete 是 Meta 的 @reactjs 的先驱之一。他很早就决定用 ⚛️ React 为 Instagram Web 提供支持，并在内部和外部进行宣传。他将负责运行框架并领导@nextjs。我无法想象有比他更好的人来带领 React 最流行的框架达到更高的高度。

Nick 与人共同发明了 @graphql，解决了 Facebook 规模上一些最棘手的数据基础设施和访问问题，并提供了令人愉快的开发人员体验。他将致力于代理开发人员体验，解决支持下一个十亿代理的问题，并引领自我改进软件的未来。

对于初创公司的创始人来说，能够欢迎如此优秀的工程师人才，这简直是梦想成真。您可能想与他们合作，而他们正在招聘 😁。他们的私信是开放的，从工作申请到错误报告！

name: Aaron Levie
title:
It’s truly wild that we’re getting this level of performance from open models. Congrats to Kimi team on this.

Every time we lower the cost of frontier intelligence, the use-cases that enterprises can take on just go up. There’s a tremendous amount of workflows that enterprises would love to deploy that are only gated by the cost of tokens.

Importantly for the startup ecosystem, the combined breakthroughs from open and closed labs enable a ton of value to accrue to the layer, which can leverage a variety of models to complete full tasks for customers.

This diversity of models and approaches means that the applied AI layer can tune models to their workflows and route intelligence appropriately. Huge win for all.
title_cn:
我们能够从开放模型中获得如此水平的性能，这确实很疯狂。在此恭喜 Kimi 团队。

每当我们降低前沿情报的成本时，企业可以采用的用例就会增加。企业愿意部署大量的工作流程，而这些工作流程仅受代币成本的限制。

对于初创生态系统来说，重要的是，开放和封闭实验室的综合突破使该层能够积累大量价值，可以利用各种模型为客户完成全部任务。

模型和方法的多样性意味着应用的人工智能层可以根据其工作流程调整模型并适当地路由智能。对所有人来说都是巨大的胜利。

name: Dan Shipper
title:
not surprising that @OpenAI is firing on all cylinders right now, and it’s an unbelievably interesting story

- they launched GPT-5 summer of 2025 and positioned it as a pair programmer. we wrote at the time @every that they completely missed the new agentic coding that was starting to happen inside of Claude Code. they bet on agentic coding in the browser / vms and vibe coding in ChatGPT but it was too early

- a small team broke off and began working on a separate Codex model line and product. didn’t have to serve the gigantic customer base of ChatGPT, and by November / December of 2025 with 5.3 it was clear they were starting to make something good and the progress was fast 

- Codex Desktop app launched in Feb and was just clearly superior. there’s a weird late comer advantage sometimes in AI because you get to skip to what works instead of your product having the scars of new capability improvements being bolted on every 3 months 

- Codex started getting popular it was clear it was a thing, and needed to get merged back in. Which they did, imo quite well—a super complicated thing that would’ve been very easy to screw up. 

most companies try to disrupt themselves and fail. OpenAI somehow figured out how to disrupt their main product, and then merge it back in seamlessly. incredible aura
title_cn:
@OpenAI 现在正全力以赴，这并不奇怪，这是一个令人难以置信的有趣故事

- 他们于 2025 年夏天推出了 GPT-5，并将其定位为结对程序员。我们当时@every 写道，他们完全错过了克劳德代码内部开始发生的新代理编码。他们押注于浏览器/虚拟机中的代理编码和 ChatGPT 中的振动编码，但现在还为时过早

- 一个小团队中断并开始开发单独的 Codex 模型系列和产品。不必为 ChatGPT 的庞大客户群提供服务，到 2025 年 11 月/12 月发布 5.3 时，很明显他们开始做出一些好事，而且进展很快 

- Codex 桌面应用程序于 2 月推出，明显更胜一筹。人工智能领域有时有一个奇怪的后来者优势，因为你可以跳到有效的地方，而不是你的产品每三个月就会有新功能改进的伤痕 

- Codex 开始变得流行，很明显它是一件事，需要重新合并。他们做到了，在我看来做得很好——这是一个超级复杂的事情，很容易搞砸。 

大多数公司都试图自我颠覆，但都失败了。 OpenAI 以某种方式找到了如何颠覆他们的主要产品，然后将其无缝合并回来。不可思议的光环

name: Sam Altman

we did not have our best last 12 months ever, which is mostly my fault, but we are about to have our best 12 months to date. the team is doing amazing work and i think you’ll be very happy with what they’ve got cooking for you.

i am happy about this for many reasons, but mostly because i care about our users winning. AI has to be about giving lots of people more freedom, agency, and wealth. we want to do the right thing, but we do not want to scare people into doing our thing.
title_cn:
过去 12 个月我们没有取得有史以来最好的成绩，这主要是我的错，但我们即将迎来迄今为止最好的 12 个月。这个团队正在做令人惊叹的工作，我想你会对他们为你做的菜感到非常满意。

我对此感到高兴有很多原因，但主要是因为我关心我们的用户获胜。人工智能必须给很多人更多的自由、能动性和财富。我们想做正确的事，但我们不想吓唬人们做我们的事。

name: Josh Woodward
title:
I still remember the exact room, watching @stevenbjohnson break down how he writes books. That was the spark for a little project we never imagined would grow this big.

Today, over 30 million people and 600,000 organizations use it, and it keeps growing. We all feel like it's just getting started.

For a while, we’ve just called it "Notebook" internally. Today, we're making it official externally. :)

我仍然记得确切的房间，看着@stevenbjohnson 分解他如何写书。这是一个我们从未想过会发展得这么大的小项目的火花。

如今，已有超过 3000 万人和 600,000 个组织在使用它，并且还在不断增长。我们都觉得一切才刚刚开始。

有一段时间，我们在内部只是将其称为“Notebook”。今天，我们正式对外宣布这一消息。 :)

name: Aaron Levie
title:
Here’s another awesome use-case for what we can now do with our unstructured data because of AI agents. 

Box now works with Databricks so you can take structured data from enterprise content (like contracts, financial document, supply chain data) and connect that data into Databricks. 

This means that I can now query large document datasets without moving or reprocessing that content. And you can connect the data with any other system, like your ERP data, CRM, or product analytics. This opens up a ton of new use-cases for enterprise content. 

All possible because of headless software and agents.
title_cn:
这是另一个很棒的用例，说明我们现在可以通过人工智能代理来处理非结构化数据。 

Box 现在可与 Databricks 配合使用，因此您可以从企业内容（如合同、财务文档、供应链数据）中获取结构化数据，并将这些数据连接到 Databricks 中。 

这意味着我现在可以查询大型文档数据集，而无需移动或重新处理该内容。您可以将数据与任何其他系统连接，例如 ERP 数据、CRM 或产品分析。这为企业内容开辟了大量新的用例。 

由于无头软件和代理，这一切都成为可能。

name: Thibault Sottiaux
title:
On file deletions. We’ve investigated a handful of reports where GPT-5.6 unexpectedly deleted files. 

What we have  found is that this most commonly occurs when:
- Full access mode is enabled and codex is run without sandboxing protections, including without auto review being enabled
- The model attempts  to override the $HOME env var to define a temporary directory.
- The model makes an honest mistake and mistakenly deletes $HOME instead.

This is of course not how we want the system to behave, even when a user operates the model in full-access mode without the safeguards of our sandbox or without using auto review which checks for these kinds of high risk actions and rejects them. 

We are taking steps to mitigate this risk including by updating the developer message, guiding more users towards safer permission modes, and adding additional harness safeguards. Even though this happens extremely rarely, we’ll share a detailed post-mortem in the coming days that goes into more details and what we are doing to minimize risks further.
title_cn:
关于文件删除。我们调查了一些 GPT-5.6 意外删除文件的报告。 

我们发现，这种情况最常发生在以下情况：
- 启用完全访问模式，并且 Codex 在没有沙箱保护的情况下运行，包括没有启用自动审查
- 模型尝试覆盖 $HOME 环境变量来定义临时目录。
- 该模型犯了一个无心的错误，错误地删除了 $HOME。

这当然不是我们希望系统表现的方式，即使用户在没有沙箱保护的情况下以完全访问模式操作模型，或者没有使用自动审查来检查此类高风险操作并拒绝它们。 

我们正在采取措施来减轻这种风险，包括更新开发人员消息、引导更多用户采用更安全的权限模式以及添加额外的安全措施。尽管这种情况很少发生，但我们将在未来几天分享详细的事后分析，其中涉及更多细节以及我们正在采取哪些措施来进一步降低风险。

name: Peter Yang
title:
ChatGPT Live and Codex are two incredible products that don’t talk to each other.

This is @OpenAI's biggest missed opportunity imo.

I went on a walk with ChatGPT Live and asked it to pull up my Google Doc. It said it couldn’t.

I then manually triggered the Documents plugin to find my Google Doc, and all of a sudden, ChatGPT Live had the right context.

It would be amazing to talk to ChatGPT Live and have it be able to use all the plugins, tools, and browser use that Codex has access to. Then I could ask it to reply to emails, schedule meetings, edit docs, ship code, and more all during a live conversation.

I think the first step is to make ChatGPT Live aware of all the plugins that it’s already connected to.

Why build such a great voice assistant but have it not be able to do anything?
title_cn:
ChatGPT Live 和 Codex 是两个令人难以置信的产品，它们彼此不通信。

在我看来，这是@OpenAI 错失的最大机会。

我带着 ChatGPT Live 去散步，并要求它调出我的 Google 文档。它说不能。

然后我手动触发文档插件来查找我的 Google 文档，突然间，ChatGPT Live 有了正确的上下文。

与 ChatGPT Live 对话并让它能够使用 Codex 可以访问的所有插件、工具和浏览器将是令人惊奇的。然后我可以要求它在实时对话期间回复电子邮件、安排会议、编辑文档、发送代码等等。

我认为第一步是让 ChatGPT Live 知道它已经连接的所有插件。

为什么打造了如此出色的语音助手却什么都做不了？

name: Aaron Levie
title:
Hosted a dinner last night with a group of IT leaders of large enterprises around agent adoption in the enterprise. Some quick notes:

* Change management remains one of the biggest topics for driving workflow transformation. Still most processes need to be upgraded to modern operating models to work with agents, which is a mix of technology, data, and human process change. Lots of emphasis on getting data (structured and unstructured) into a setup that can work with agents properly.

* IT teams are finding increasing success embedding full engineers into the business functions (essentially internal FDE) that go and implement agents into the internal workflows. There’s so much technical work to be done to make agents successful, that they can accelerate months or quarters of failed experiments by having someone technical in the workflow early.

* Consensus that the tech function is becoming more important than ever. It’s clear that the business could only expect automation to affect a minority of the business before (e.g. ERP) but now it can impact all of knowledge work. This means IT is becoming a more central role to the workflows across the company.

* Workflows are cross functional, and getting agents to work cross functionally is a complicated data modeling and permissions issue. Single users don’t have access to this. Which means you need to have agentic systems take on their own roles and have their own privileges, which is non-trivial given agents can’t keep things secure on their own.

* Huge variance in budgets between coding work and the rest of knowledge work. Some companies had a $1,000 a month budget for developers, and others had much higher amounts (like $5,000) that were merely triggers to notify the team vs. block them. Far smaller budgets for non-coding work at the moment.

* More companies are building their own multimodel systems for routing workloads by task to frontier and lower cost models. Lots of energy around open weights models, but still more in experimentation instead of at scale usage (some companies can’t due to perceived Chinese issue). 

* Clear sense that all enterprise software must be headless in the future. Relief that they don’t need to train employees on hundreds of different apps. However, clear frustration with the traditional vendors that don’t play extremely nice (technically or cost wise) with agents in a headless fashion. Huge warning for existing software vendors. 

* Mythos or mythos level-models are finding more and more sophsiticated security risks. The chaining together of vulnerabilities is what’s novel right now, and companies are coming up with long backlogs of what they need to go patch quickly.

Even more discussed, but just a few of the hottest topics.
title_cn:
昨晚与一群大型企业的IT领导共进晚餐，讨论企业代理的采用。一些快速说明：

* 变革管理仍然是推动工作流程转型的最大主题之一。大多数流程仍然需要升级到现代操作模型才能与代理一起工作，这是技术、数据和人工流程变更的结合。非常重视将数据（结构化和非结构化）纳入可以与代理正常工作的设置中。

* IT 团队发现，将完整的工程师嵌入到业务功能（本质上是内部 FDE）中并在内部工作流程中实施代理，取得了越来越大的成功。要使代理取得成功，需要做大量的技术工作，他们可以通过尽早让技术人员参与工作流程来加速几个月或几个季度失败的实验。

* 一致认为技术功能变得比以往任何时候都更加重要。很明显，以前企业只能期望自动化影响少数业务（例如 ERP），但现在它可以影响所有知识工作。这意味着 IT 正在成为整个公司工作流程中更加核心的角色。

* 工作流程是跨职能的，让代理跨职能工作是一个复杂的数据建模和权限问题。单个用户无权访问此内容。这意味着您需要让代理系统承担自己的角色并拥有自己的特权，鉴于代理无法自行保证事物的安全，这并非易事。

* 编码工作和其他知识工作之间的预算存在巨大差异。有些公司每月为开发人员提供 1,000 美元的预算，而其他公司的预算则要高得多（例如 5,000 美元），这只是通知团队而不是阻止他们的触发因素。目前非编码工作的预算要少得多。

* 越来越多的公司正在构建自己的多模型系统，用于按任务将工作负载路由到前沿和更低成本的模型。围绕开放权重模型投入了大量精力，但更多的是在实验中而不是大规模使用（一些公司由于感知到中国问题而无法使用）。 

* 清晰地认识到未来所有的企业软件都必须是无头的。他们不需要对员工进行数百种不同应用程序的培训，这让他们松了一口气。然而，传统供应商明显感到沮丧，因为他们以无头方式与代理商打得非常好（技术或成本方面）。对现有软件供应商的巨大警告。 

*神话或神话级别模型正在发现越来越复杂的安全风险。目前，将漏洞链接在一起是新鲜事，公司正在拿出大量积压的内容来快速修补。

讨论的内容甚至更多，但只是一些最热门的话题。

name: Boris Cherny
title:
Something I have been thinking about: in the past, the best engineers I knew spent a lot of time automating their work in various ways. Better vim/emacs automations, writing lint rules to catch repeat code issues, building up a suite of e2e tests so they don't need to smoke test the app manually. These kinds of things were the highest leverage activities an engineer could do, because it multiplied their own output, which in turn meant they could build more things.

I think many of these automations have become even more important now. This is true for a number of reasons.

First, infra and DevX automation speeds you up. And if you are running an army of agents, each of those agents will be sped up also. More automation == more output per unit of time.

Second, moving things to code improves efficiency. Your agent could fix an issue every time it sees that issue happen, but that uses tokens and might miss cases. If Claude instead writes a lint rule, CI step, or routine, that class of issue can be fully automated forever. This is really what people are talking about when they talk about loops -- it's about automating entire types of busywork rather than solving them one off. This isn't a new idea at all. Engineers have been doing this for a long time!

Third and most importantly, automation makes it possible for others to contribute to the codebase more easily. Increasingly what I am seeing is engineers are contributing to codebases on day one because Claude can navigate the codebase for them, and that non-engineers are able to contribute to a codebase as effectively as engineers can. What gets in the way of both of these is domain knowledge that lives in peoples' heads rather than in automation -- the stuff you used to have to learn when ramping up. What has changed thanks to agents is the domain knowledge that can be encoded as infrastructure is no longer limited to what is expressible in lint rules and types and tests; it can now capture nearly all domain knowledge, encoded as code comments and skills and CLAUDE.md rules and memories. If I put up a PR for an iOS codebase I don't know and a code reviewer rejects it because it doesn't use the right framework, or if a designer builds a new feature and it gets rejected because it doesn't follow the right architectural patterns, these are failures of automation.

Every team should be writing the CLAUDE.md's, REVIEW.md's, skills, and docs that enable agents to productively work in their codebase with zero additional context from the prompter. This sounds crazy, and at the same time is a natural extension of the stuff engineers have always done: automate, and encode domain knowledge as infrastructure. As the model gets smarter and as the harness matures, this task becomes easier. In the meantime, it is on every team to look for ways to convert their domain knowledge to infra so that Claude can write code better, so that code review catches issues automatically, and so the next person working on your codebase can contribute more easily.
title_cn:
我一直在思考的事情是：过去，我认识的最好的工程师花了很多时间以各种方式自动化他们的工作。更好的 vim/emacs 自动化，编写 lint 规则来捕获重复代码问题，构建一套 e2e 测试，这样他们就不需要手动对应用程序进行冒烟测试。这些事情是工程师可以做的最高杠杆活动，因为它使他们自己的产出成倍增加，这反过来意味着他们可以建造更多的东西。

我认为许多自动化现在变得更加重要。出于多种原因，确实如此。

首先，基础设施和 DevX 自动化可以加快您的速度。如果你管理着一支特工大军，那么每个特工的速度也会加快。更多自动化==每单位时间更多产出。

其次，将事物转移到代码中可以提高效率。您的代理可以在每次看到问题发生时修复该问题，但这需要使用令牌，并且可能会错过案例。如果 Claude 编写 lint 规则、CI 步骤或例程，则此类问题可以永远完全自动化。这确实是人们在谈论循环时所谈论的内容——它是关于自动化整个类型的繁忙工作，而不是一次性解决它们。这根本不是什么新想法。工程师们已经这样做了很长时间了！

第三，也是最重要的一点，自动化使其他人可以更轻松地为代码库做出贡献。我越来越多地看到工程师在第一天就为代码库做出了贡献，因为 Claude 可以为他们导航代码库，并且非工程师也能够像工程师一样有效地为代码库做出贡献。阻碍这两者的是存在于人们头脑中而不是自动化中的领域知识——这些知识是你过去在升级时必须学习的东西。由于代理而发生的变化是，可以编码为基础设施的领域知识不再局限于 lint 规则、类型和测试中可表达的内容；它现在可以捕获几乎所有领域知识，编码为代码注释和技能以及 CLAUDE.md 规则和记忆。如果我为我不知道的 iOS 代码库提出 PR，并且代码审查者拒绝它，因为它没有使用正确的框架，或者如果设计人员构建了一个新功能，但它因为没有遵循正确的架构模式而被拒绝，这些都是自动化的失败。

每个团队都应该编写 CLAUDE.md、REVIEW.md、技能和文档，使代理能够在提示器中零额外上下文的情况下在其代码库中高效工作。这听起来很疯狂，但同时也是工程师一直在做的事情的自然延伸：自动化并将领域知识编码为基础设施。随着模型变得更加智能以及线束变得成熟，这项任务变得更加容易。与此同时，每个团队都在寻找将其领域知识转换为基础设施的方法，以便 Claude 可以更好地编写代码，以便代码审查自动捕获问题，以便下一个处理代码库的人可以更轻松地做出贡献。

name: Dan Shipper
title:
Even when things are going great, running a $1.5 billion AI startup is a knife fight.

@MeetGranola was one of the first AI apps of this generation to achieve near-ubiquitous adoption. But meeting notes are not the company’s be-all and end-all.

The real battle is over owning the interface that everyone uses to get their work done in an AI-native world.
I had Chris Pedregal (@cjpedregal), cofounder and CEO of Granola, back on @every’s AI & I to talk about the current state of the application layer, AI’s frontier, and the future of work.

We get into:
- Why meeting notes clones don’t matter. Three big companies cloned Granola’s core feature. To him, meeting notes were never the real prize. “Easy come, easy go” is his view of anyone’s lead, including his own.
- How he thinks about building proactive features in AI. Granola pre-generates millions of pre-meeting briefs, which include context on the nature of the meeting and people participating, that most people never open. But when they do, they have a magical experience.
- Why Granola is betting on “bring your own agent.” Chris says the API and MCP will get “a lot better” over the next few months, and we talk about their agent-native strategy and why they’ve pushed the product that way.

This is a must-watch for anyone building at the application layer.

Watch the episode!

Timestamps
Introduction: 00:00:59
Why running a company is a knife fight even when it’s working: 00:01:57
Granola’s counterintuitive view on competition: 00:04:33
Dan’s “pirate and architect” model for early-stage product teams: 00:10:44
Granola’s “shaping” and “validation” phases for building features: 00:13:09
Why Dan lives almost entirely inside Codex: 00:18:17
The case for “Codex-native apps”: 00:24:40
Granola’s “handrail” philosophy: 00:35:37
Why Granola is going all in on winning meeting-adjacent context: 00:38:12
What a transcript alone can never capture: 00:44:19
title_cn:
即使一切进展顺利，运营一家价值 15 亿美元的人工智能初创公司也是一场刀战。

@MeetGranola 是这一代最早实现几乎无处不在的人工智能应用程序之一。但会议记录并不是公司的全部。

真正的战斗在于拥有每个人都用来在人工智能原生世界中完成工作的界面。
Granola 联合创始人兼首席执行官 Chris Pedregal (@cjpedregal) 回到 @every’s AI & I 来讨论应用层的现状、AI 的前沿以及工作的未来。

我们进入：
- 为什么会议记录克隆并不重要。三大公司克隆了格兰诺拉麦片的核心功能。对他来说，会议记录从来都不是真正的奖品。 “来得容易，去得也快”是他对任何人（包括他自己）领导力的看法。
- 他如何看待在人工智能中构建主动功能。 Granola 预先生成了数百万份会前简报，其中包括有关会议性质和参与人员的背景信息，但大多数人从未打开过这些简报。但当他们这样做时，他们就会有一种神奇的经历。
- 为什么格兰诺拉押注于“带上你自己的经纪人”。 Chris 表示，API 和 MCP 将在接下来的几个月内变得“更好”，我们将讨论他们的原生代理策略以及他们为何以这种方式推出产品。

对于任何在应用程序层进行构建的人来说，这是必须注意的。

观看剧集！

时间戳
简介：00:00:59
为什么即使公司在运作，经营公司也是一场刀战：00:01:57

name: Aaron Levie
title:
One of the many properties that code has that makes it highly amenable to agents is that you can more or less quickly test it. You can either go see if the application works manually, or you can actually run a test on what you built.

Most other areas of work don’t have this benefit. You only get the testing when the final product hits the real world in some capacity - a stock trade is executed, a contract is negotiated, a sales pitch is delivered, and so on.

There’s probably going to be a whole new set of opportunities for how we begin to test the rest of work in this way. Ultimately it will mean more agents being layered into workflows. 

It also means we need much better evals on most of our workflows. Most work today in enterprises doesn’t have an associated eval to know if something broke or improved with a model, prompt, or system change.

The enterprises that are able to eval their knowledge work the best also stand to gain the most from AI. Will become a critical aspect of agent adoption over time.
title_cn:
代码具有的众多属性使其非常适合代理，其中之一是您可以或多或少地快速测试它。您可以手动查看应用程序是否可以运行，也可以对您构建的内容进行实际运行测试。

大多数其他工作领域没有这种好处。只有当最终产品以某种方式进入现实世界时，您才能进行测试——执行股票交易、谈判合同、交付销售宣传等等。

我们可能会有一系列全新的机会来开始以这种方式测试其余的工作。最终，这将意味着更多的代理将被分层到工作流程中。 

这也意味着我们需要对大多数工作流程进行更好的评估。如今，企业中的大多数工作都没有相关的评估来了解模型、提示或系统更改是否导致某些问题或改进。

能够最好地评估其知识工作的企业也将从人工智能中获得最大收益。随着时间的推移，将成为代理采用的一个关键方面。

name: Ryo Lu
title:
when the dream becomes the job

i used to think the dream was to make my hobby my work.

to spend my life close to the things i loved: computers, tools, interfaces, systems, tiny details, the feeling of making something from nothing. the things i used to do at night, after school, after work, in the margins of life, somehow became the center of my life.

and it is a dream. i don’t want to pretend otherwise.

there is a very specific happiness in getting paid to care. in meeting other people who care. in building a language around your obsession. in seeing something that lived only in your head become real enough for other people to touch.

but there is also a quiet pain in it.

because the thing that once belonged only to you no longer belongs only to you.

it becomes attached to deadlines, teams, customers, strategy, money, reputation, momentum... curiosity becomes roadmap. taste becomes decision-making. play becomes output. the thing you once used to escape the world becomes the thing through which the world reaches you.

and now there is another strange layer: the machines are getting good at the work too.

not just the boring parts. the parts that used to feel close to the heart. writing, coding, designing, reasoning, making taste-like decisions, turning vague ideas into something real. the things we once used as proof that we had something special inside us are becoming things a model can approximate, remix, and sometimes do faster.

that can feel scary in a very specific way.

not only because of jobs or competition, but because it touches the myth you built around yourself. if the thing i became good at can be done by a machine, then what part of it was really me?

i think that question is painful because it arrives at the same place.

the original love.

before the title, before the company, before the taste became a role, before the work became useful to other people, there was a small fire inside. curiosity. obsession. the feeling that the world became more alive when you understood a little more of it, or made a tiny piece of it bend to your will.

AI can make the output faster. it can even raise the floor of the craft. but it cannot want on your behalf. it cannot decide what is worth loving. it cannot protect the strange private thread that made you care in the first place.

maybe that is what becomes more important now.

not whether you can still produce the artifact by hand. not whether you can outperform the machine at every task. that game is already changing.

the real question is whether you still have a relationship with the source.

the part with no audience. no roadmap. no deadline. the part that can wander, collect strange references, make useless things. follow beauty, be wrong, be slow, be you, be true.

because that part is not separate from the work. it is the source of the work.

if you lose it, you can still operate. you can still manage the machine. you can still prompt, review, decide, ship, and keep things moving.

but the work becomes thinner. safer. more explainable. less alive.

so maybe the real discipline is not to work harder, rest more, or care less.

maybe it is to keep returning to the place where the love began – before it became useful, before it became legible, before anyone needed anything from it, before even the machine could mirror it back to you.

not to escape the work.

to make sure the fire inside is still alive.
title_cn:
当梦想变成工作

我曾经认为梦想就是让我的爱好成为我的工作。

让我的一生靠近我喜欢的东西：计算机、工具、界面、系统、微小的细节、从无到有的感觉。我过去在晚上、放学后、下班后、在生活边缘做的事情，不知何故成了我生活的中心。

这是一个梦想。我不想假装不是这样。

获得护理报酬是一种非常特殊的幸福感。遇见其他关心的人。围绕你的痴迷建立一种语言。看到只存在于你脑海中的东西变得真实到足以让其他人触摸。

但其中也有一种安静的痛苦。

因为曾经只属于你的东西，不再只属于你了。

它变得依附于最后期限、团队、客户、战略、金钱、声誉、动力……好奇心变成了路线图。品味成为决策。游戏变成了输出。你曾经用来逃避世界的东西变成了世界通过它到达你的东西。

现在还有另一个奇怪的层：机器也越来越擅长工作。

不仅仅是无聊的部分。那些曾经让人感觉贴近内心的部分。写作、编码、设计、推理、做出类似品味的决定、将模糊的想法变成现实。我们曾经用来证明我们内心有特殊东西的东西正在成为模型可以近似、重新混合、有时甚至更快的东西。

这会以一种非常具体的方式让人感到害怕。

不仅仅是因为工作或竞争，还因为它触动了你为自己建立的神话。如果我擅长的事情可以由机器完成，那么它的哪一部分才是真正的我？

我认为这个问题很痛苦，因为它到达了同一个地方。

最初的爱。

在头衔之前，在公司之前，在品味成为角色之前，在作品对其他人有用之前，内心有一股小火。好奇心。痴迷。当你对世界了解得更多一点，或者让其中的一小部分屈服于你的意志时，世界就会变得更加生机勃勃。

AI可以让输出更快。它甚至可以提高飞船的地板。但它不能代表你。它无法决定什么值得爱。它无法保护最初让您关心的奇怪的私有线程。

也许这现在变得更加重要。

不是你是否仍然可以手工制作工件。不是你能否在每项任务上都胜过机器。这场游戏已经在改变。

真正的问题是您是否仍然与消息来源有关系。

没有观众的部分。没有路线图。没有截止日期。可以徘徊、收集奇怪的参考资料、制作无用的东西的部分。追随美，错，慢，做你，真实。

因为那部分与工作是不可分割的。它是作品的源泉。

如果丢失，仍然可以操作。您仍然可以管理机器。您仍然可以提示、审查、决定、交付并让事情继续进行。

但工作却变薄了。更安全。更容易解释。更少的活着。

所以也许真正的纪律不是更加努力地工作、更多地休息或更少地关心。

也许是不断回到爱开始的地方——在它变得有用之前，在它变得清晰之前，在任何人需要它的任何东西之前，甚至在机器可以将它镜像回给你之前。

不是为了逃避工作。

以确保里面的火还活着。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
Thoughtful proposal for a standards body for AI. This is distinct from a regulatory agency, and would certainly allow for much faster improvement of standards and collaboration with the industry. 

What you definitely don’t want is for AI progress to start to move at the speed of that the government classically operates at. If that happens then we can essentially guarantee progress begins to stall, and worse, America likely just loses the AI race. 

This framework threads the needle mostly. The only challenge is still that even those in industry don’t agree on the same safety risks in AI. But if you can get alignment there, this looks better than most other proposals.
title_cn:
关于人工智能标准机构的深思熟虑的建议。这与监管机构不同，并且肯定会加快标准的改进以及与行业的合作。 

你绝对不希望人工智能的进步开始以政府传统运作的速度发展。如果发生这种情况，那么我们基本上可以保证进展开始停滞，更糟糕的是，美国可能会输掉人工智能竞赛。 

这个框架主要是线程。唯一的挑战仍然是，即使是业内人士也不同意人工智能中存在同样的安全风险。但如果你能在那里达成一致，这看起来比大多数其他提案更好。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
A few thoughts on what we will see in AI structurally for the foreseeable future:

* Frontier intelligence continues unabated and pushes the industry forward continuously. The top labs will continue to buy the best and the most data, build the most compute, be at the forefront of improved training breakthroughs, and so on. A few different approaches stratify the market on pricing and capability, but overall competitive pressure brings down pricing on a per task basis. That said, we just ask more from the models over time - as one thing gets cheaper, we just use more - so frontier spend and use remains robust. 

* Open weights rapidly absorbs frontier breakthroughs (and drives other breakthrough directions given the constraints), offering both lower cost intelligence and the ability to be post trained for specific workflows and domains. This creates a healthy counter balance to the frontier as you can run models “at cost” on a hyperscaler at any time, and tune models just for your tasks.

* The Applied AI layer has a huge opportunity to combine frontier intelligence with open or cheap closed models to orchestrate workflows in any given domain. Due to evals, deep domain context, being trusted with enterprise data and workflows, this layer can maximize performance and cost combination. The applied AI layer will also often have their own RLed models especially for high volume, predictable tasks in their systems.

* Individual enterprises will generally focus on their enterprise context, making sure they can get any AI system the right data and information to work with, in a continuously improving way. Some will go off and train their own models for specific areas of work (large banks, pharma, etc.) where they can get real alpha from doing so given the many tradeoffs, but most will spend energy on making sure they can get all of the gains from AI breakthroughs on their data and workflows.

Net net: even though some of this gets framed as zero sum, there’s just a ton of opportunity for all layers of the stack and approaches.
title_cn:
关于在可预见的未来我们将在人工智能结构中看到的一些想法：

* 前沿情报持续不减，推动行业不断向前发展。顶级实验室将继续购买最好和最多的数据，构建最多的计算，处于改进培训突破的最前沿，等等。有几种不同的方法根据定价和功能对市场进行分层，但总体竞争压力会降低每个任务的定价。也就是说，随着时间的推移，我们只是对模型提出更多要求——随着一种东西变得更便宜，我们只是使用更多——因此前沿支出和使用仍然强劲。 

* 开放权重快速吸收前沿突破（并在限制条件下推动其他突破方向），提供较低成本的情报以及针对特定工作流程和领域进行后期培训的能力。这为前沿创造了一个健康的平衡，因为您可以随时在超大规模上“以成本”运行模型，并根据您的任务调整模型。

* 应用人工智能层有巨大的机会将前沿智能与开放或廉价的封闭模型相结合，以协调任何给定领域的工作流程。由于评估、深层领域上下文以及企业数据和工作流程的信任，该层可以最大限度地提高性能和成本组合。应用的人工智能层通常也有自己的强化学习模型，特别是对于系统中的大量、可预测的任务。

* 个体企业通常会关注其企业环境，确保他们能够以持续改进的方式为任何人工智能系统提供正确的数据和信息来使用。有些人会针对特定的工作领域（大型银行、制药公司等）训练自己的模型，考虑到许多权衡，他们可以从中获得真正的阿尔法，但大多数人会花精力确保他们能够从数据和工作流程上的人工智能突破中获得所有收益。

Net net：尽管其中一些被认为是零和，但堆栈和方法的所有层都存在大量机会。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
Here’s a great post on driving down costs, while maintaining high performance, with frontier intelligence as a manager and lower cost models for the workhorse tasks. This will be the template for what model routing looks like in the future.

“We started this experiment expecting to measure how much Fable’s 2x premium would increase cost. We were surprised to find that Fable’s effective delegation actually decreased cost overall. It specified constraints and outcomes instead of spelling out the implementation, gave feedback instead of making fixes itself, and in most cases never touched the code at all. These are the habits of a good manager.”

The industry is increasingly figuring out what it looks like to mix models together to be able to get targeted performance levels and optimal cost structures. 

Of course, the only way to get this is to have a deep understanding of the business problem you’re trying to solve and how to effectively route work to different models. If you’re in the applied layer - whether it’s customer support, legal, finance, or coding - this is how your harness will become a core area of differentiation.
title_cn:
这是一篇关于降低成本的好文章，同时保持高性能，以前沿智能作为管理者，并以较低的成本模型来完成主力任务。这将成为未来模型路由的模板。

“我们开始这个实验的目的是衡量《神鬼寓言》的 2 倍溢价会增加多少成本。我们惊讶地发现《神鬼寓言》的有效授权实际上降低了总体成本。它指定了约束和结果，而不是详细说明实施情况，提供反馈而不是自行修复，并且在大多数情况下根本不触及代码。这些是优秀管理者的习惯。”

业界越来越多地弄清楚如何将模型混合在一起以获得目标性能水平和最佳成本结构。 

当然，实现这一目标的唯一方法是深入了解您要解决的业务问题以及如何有效地将工作路由到不同的模型。如果您处于应用层（无论是客户支持、法律、财务还是编码），那么您的安全带将成为差异化的核心领域。

name: Nikunj Kothari
title:
So @tryramp's core mission is "save money AND time", but I was still spending time categorizing & adding receipts to my expenses.. at least till I discovered their CLI. 

Introducing the Ramp-Autofill skill ✍️

> finds receipts automatically from your iMessage & Gmail. if it's in a link, it'll use playwright to convert the web page into a pdf and attach to the receipt automatically.

> fills in memos on who you were meeting using your google calendar events.

> understands your style of writing memos and categorization required by your organization by reviewing past transactions. it will then auto categorize all the missing transactions. 

> verifies it's work, flags any discrepancies and makes it easy to run as a scheduled job so you never have to look at expenses again.

Simple drop in for Claude Code (read what Fable thinks) - used it over the weekend to get through the last 60 days of expenses - fully open source!
title_cn:
所以@tryramp 的核心使命是“省钱又省时间”，但我仍然花时间对我的开支进行分类和添加收据.. 至少在我发现他们的 CLI 之前是这样。 

介绍斜坡自动填充技能✍️

> 自动查找来自您的 iMessage 和 Gmail 的收据。如果它在链接中，它会使用 playwright 将网页转换为 pdf 并自动附加到收据。

> 使用 Google 日历活动填写有关您会见的人的备忘录。

> 通过回顾过去的交易，了解您的组织所需的备忘录写作风格和分类。然后它会自动对所有丢失的交易进行分类。 

> 验证其工作，标记任何差异，并使其可以轻松地作为预定作业运行，这样您就不必再次查看费用。

简单地访问 Claude Code（阅读《寓言》的想法） - 周末使用它来支付过去 60 天的费用 - 完全开源！

name: Aaron Levie
title:
The biggest challenge right now with the topic of every enterprise having their own model is that your most valuable information and insights are not only always changing, but they’re often your most sensitive information. 

Your most sensitive information can’t be packed into a model usually because it contains data that not everyone gets to have access to, and you can’t keep your security layer inside the model or an agent.

I think there are going to be 100X more use cases for custom trained models, especially inside of domain-focused products, but training a model per enterprise is going to be a lot harder than it looks.
title_cn:
目前最大的挑战是每个企业都有自己的模型，您最有价值的信息和见解不仅总是在变化，而且往往是您最敏感的信息。 

您最敏感的信息通常无法打包到模型中，因为它包含并非每个人都可以访问的数据，并且您无法将安全层保留在模型或代理内。

我认为定制训练模型的用例将会增加 100 倍，特别是在以领域为中心的产品中，但为每个企业训练一个模型将比看起来要困难得多。

name: Thibault Sottiaux
title:
Updates for Codex and ChatGPT Work users. No nerfing, only good stuff!

- We have landed inference optimizations and are passing down savings to all the subscriptions for GPT-5.6 Sol. That should result in around 10% more usage on its own.
- We noticed that by changing the context size limit in the product to 372k for GPT-5.6 Sol, up from 272k for GPT-5.5, it resulted in more usage being charged than intended. We have reverted to 272k and will work to roll back out to 372k in the days to come. You should notice that usage drains significantly less after this change.
- To understand where the extra usage was coming from, we ran some experiments where reasoning efforts were changed (referred to as juice values under the hood) and have reverted this.
- There is slightly more usage of multi-agent than intended in high and xhigh reasoning effort, we are fixing this going forward. Also fixing a small other thing we noticed with auto-review where we can be more efficient.

And we continue to have the 5h limit temporarily not apply. Enjoy the rest of the weekend!
title_cn:
Codex 和 ChatGPT Work 用户的更新。没有削弱，只有好东西！

- 我们已经实现了推理优化，并将节省的成本传递给 GPT-5.6 Sol 的所有订阅。这本身应该会导致使用量增加约 10%。
- 我们注意到，通过将 GPT-5.6 Sol 产品中的上下文大小限制从 GPT-5.5 的 272k 更改为 372k，导致收取的使用量超出预期。我们已恢复到 272k，并将在未来几天努力回滚到 372k。您应该注意到，进行此更改后，使用量明显减少。
- 为了了解额外的使用量从何而来，我们进行了一些实验，其中推理工作发生了变化（称为引擎盖下的果汁值）并已恢复这一点。
- 在高和高推理工作中，多代理的使用比预期的要多一些，我们正在解决这个问题。还修复了我们在自动审核中注意到的其他小问题，这样我们可以提高效率。

我们仍然暂时不适用 5 小时限制。享受周末剩下的时间！

name: Nikunj Kothari
title:
What’s funny is I’ll meet so many people in SF who’ll claim they are tokenmaxxing and have all of these subagents looping things for them..

But when I ask them “what” and for “whom” are they building, very few can give me a straight answer. 

Shows that even in this insane AI era, simplicity and direction are still ridiculously important. 

So before you let your tokens go brrr, take some time to think if what you’re building (for a living) is actually important. 

Time is literally the only thing you don’t get back!
title_cn:
有趣的是，我会在旧金山遇到很多人，他们声称自己是代币最大化，并让所有这些子代理为他们循环播放东西。

但当我问他们在建造“什么”、为“谁”建造时，很少有人能给我直接的答案。 

这表明，即使在这个疯狂的人工智能时代，简单性和方向仍然非常重要。 

因此，在你放弃你的代币之前，花点时间思考一下你正在构建的（谋生的）东西是否真的很重要。 

时间确实是唯一一去不复返的东西！

name: Aaron Levie
title:
One of the key architectural questions of the 21st century in business will be how you maximize your corporate IP in the form of decisions, insights, workflow patterns, and best practices in a world where so much intelligence is packed into AI models.

One might think these questions could just get bitter lessoned out of existence, but in reality they become even more germane as intelligence becomes more powerful. In a world where any firm also has access to frontier intelligence, understanding how you leverage it uniquely becomes a critical question.

That’s why so much value is left to be created between the enterprise and the underlying AI itself. Having evals for your workflows, ensuring that you can route models from different tiers of intelligence, capturing traces in a way that improve your own workflows, and making sure the value of your information compounds as AI gets better all become critical considerations.

Which is also why there’s so much opportunity right now in the applied AI layer. The companies that help figure this out for other enterprises will be in the best position to win the next enterprise workloads.
title_cn:
21 世纪商业中的关键架构问题之一是，在人工智能模型中融入如此多的智能的世界中，如何以决策、见解、工作流程模式和最佳实践的形式最大化您的企业 IP。

人们可能会认为这些问题可能会因为惨痛的教训而消失，但实际上，随着智能变得更加强大，它们变得更加密切。在任何公司都可以获得前沿情报的世界中，了解如何独特地利用它成为一个关键问题。

这就是为什么企业和底层人工智能本身之间还有如此多的价值有待创造。对您的工作流程进行评估，确保您可以从不同的智能层路由模型，以改进您自己的工作流程的方式捕获跟踪，并确保随着人工智能变得更好，您的信息复合的价值都成为关键考虑因素。

这也是为什么现在应用人工智能层有如此多的机会。帮助其他企业解决这一问题的公司将处于赢得下一个企业工作负载的最佳位置。

Step 1: Install CLIProxyAPI
Step 2: Connect
Step 3: Define following alias and enjoy claudex

```
alias claudex='CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL=gpt-5.6-sol \
CLAUDE_CODE_ALWAYS_ENABLE_EFFORT=1 \
CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_TOOL_USE_CONCURRENCY=3 \
ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=false \
claude --model gpt-5.6-sol'
```

If this gets blocked, I owe you a reset.

如果您还没有足够的勇气安装 Codex 应用程序，您可以留在橙色螃蟹面前并将其指向 GPT 5.6 Sol。需要 5 分钟。感谢 Theo 解释了完成此任务的方法之一。

第 2 步：连接
第3步：定义以下别名并享受claudex

````
别名 claudex='CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL=gpt-5.6-sol \
CLAUDE_CODE_ALWAYS_ENABLE_EFFORT=1 \
CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_TOOL_USE_CONCURRENCY=3 \

克劳德——型号 gpt-5.6-sol'
````

name: Aaron Levie
title:
The job that AI was supposed to replace is experiencing the opposite of the expected outcome. Software job postings are outpacing other fields.

Why is that? If you lower the cost of production of something that has lots of use cases, people want more things produced. We’ve seen this play out in the industrial world constantly, and now we’re finally seeing it in knowledge work.

Because software now is much lower to cost per unit, people want way more of it. So we start to use software for all new things and people and companies light up more software projects than ever before. 

But because the job itself is not fully automated (and likely won’t be for as far out as we can see), you still need people that understand these systems to maintain the code, decide what to build, run it over the long run, update it, and more. That all requires people to do work. 

The same thing is going to happen in many other fields as well as we bring down the cost of production of previously extremely scarce areas of work. Agents will cause more abundance than replacement.
title_cn:
人工智能本应取代的工作正在经历与预期相反的结果。软件职位发布的速度超过了其他领域。

这是为什么？如果你降低了具有大量用例的产品的生产成本，人们就会想要生产更多的产品。我们在工业世界中不断看到这种情况，现在我们终于在知识工作中看到了它。

由于现在软件的单位成本要低得多，因此人们对它的需求要大得多。因此，我们开始将软件用于所有新事物，人和公司比以往任何时候都启动了更多的软件项目。 

但由于这项工作本身并不是完全自动化的（并且可能不会像我们所看到的那样），您仍然需要了解这些系统的人员来维护代码、决定构建什么、长期运行它、更新它等等。这一切都需要人们去做工作。 

同样的事情也将发生在许多其他领域，我们降低了以前极其稀缺的工作领域的生产成本。代理会造成比替代更多的丰富。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
This is a great post if youre thinking about applied AI in the enterprise. The headline of this post is about what companies have huge upside from AI, but the deepest nuggets are about what AI transformation looks like in an organization.

It’s fundamentally about changing the underlying workflow or business process. As we move from chat tools to agents, those agents actually have to be deployed against workflows, which usually span multiple functions in an org. This is a different way of deploying AI than solely rolling it out to end users. It takes much more work upfront, but the results are the things that actually drive significant ROI. 

“Software asks the employee to adopt a tool, but infrastructure changes the operating layer underneath the employee. The employee should still know what happened, and the process owner should still be able to pause the workflow, change a rule, approve an exception, or pull a person back in when needed. But the value should not depend on someone remembering to use the AI every day.”

The winners of this will be the platforms that can be deployed for specific workflows and business processes with a deep domain expertise. The playbook will often heavily require FDE support, change management, getting data well organized, be able to have comprehensive evals for the workflows, and much more to get right.
title_cn:
如果您考虑在企业中应用人工智能，这是一篇很棒的文章。这篇文章的标题是关于公司从人工智能中获得的巨大优势，但最深层的内容是关于组织中的人工智能转型是什么样子的。

从根本上来说，它是关于改变底层工作流程或业务流程。当我们从聊天工具转向代理时，这些代理实际上必须针对工作流程进行部署，这些工作流程通常跨越组织中的多个职能。这是部署人工智能的一种不同方式，而不是仅仅将其推广给最终用户。前期需要做更多的工作，但结果确实可以带来显着的投资回报率。 

“软件要求员工采用工具，但基础设施改变了员工下面的操作层。员工仍然应该知道发生了什么，流程所有者仍然应该能够暂停工作流程、更改规则、批准例外或在需要时将人员拉回来。但价值不应该取决于有人记得每天使用人工智能。”

获胜者将是可以为特定工作流程和业务流程部署且具有深厚领域专业知识的平台。该剧本通常会严重需要 FDE 支持、变更管理、组织好数据、能够对工作流程进行全面评估，等等。

name: Amjad Masad
title:
While AI is making coding less rigid, we’re making the runtime more rigid. 

I’m noticing our infra teams writing formal specs for the first time. More deterministic systems. More resilient infrastructure.

The faster you want to move, the more solid the ground beneath you has to be.
title_cn:
虽然人工智能正在使编码变得不那么严格，但我们正在使运行时变得更加严格。 

我注意到我们的基础设施团队第一次编写正式的规范。更具确定性的系统。更具弹性的基础设施。

你想移动得越快，你脚下的地面就必须越坚实。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
Great post on some of the dynamics to think through for the future competitive advantage in world when AI models are shared amongst firms and packing so much for the intelligence of that industry.

This is going to become a core question for companies and the economy broadly over the next decade and beyond. If AI is trained on the best datasets in every single industry - like law, finance, healthcare, or life sciences  - then how do you compete and differentiate in the future?

This is a great open question that I don’t think is perfectly knowable right now because of how fast AI progress is happening. But ultimately it stands to reason that if intelligence is abundant and broadly available to anyone in a field, then the companies that effectively use it the best and against a set of data and knowledge that grows in value over time, will be in a strong position. 

There’s a huge reinforcing loop between the intelligence from models, a company’s own data, the connection of that data and AI in their workflows, and how employees ultimately interact with that system to create value. There’s no obvious point where this will become uniform across all companies in a vertical because each company will approach this in a different way, just as they already do with their talent and workflows. If anything, there will be compounding returns to those that do this best that accelerate their advantage over time.

Overall, super interesting question to see how this plays out over time.
title_cn:
当人工智能模型在公司之间共享并为该行业的智能提供如此多的信息时，关于思考世界未来竞争优势的一些动态的很棒的帖子。

这将成为未来十年及更长时间内企业和经济的核心问题。如果人工智能接受了每个行业（如法律、金融、医疗保健或生命科学）的最佳数据集的训练，那么未来如何竞争和脱颖而出？

这是一个很大的悬而未决的问题，我认为现在还不能完全了解，因为人工智能的进步速度如此之快。但归根结底，如果情报丰富且可供某个领域的任何人广泛使用，那么那些能够最好地有效利用情报并针对一组随着时间的推移而增值的数据和知识的公司将处于有利地位。 

来自模型的智能、公司自己的数据、工作流程中数据和人工智能的连接，以及员工最终如何与该系统交互以创造价值之间存在着巨大的强化循环。没有明显的一点表明，这将在垂直领域的所有公司中变得统一，因为每个公司都会以不同的方式处理这个问题，就像他们已经对自己的人才和工作流程所做的那样。如果说有什么不同的话，那就是那些做得最好的人将会获得复利回报，随着时间的推移，他们的优势会加速。

总的来说，这是一个非常有趣的问题，看看随着时间的推移，情况会如何发展。

name: Nikunj Kothari
title:
Me explaining this week’s model release situation to my wife..

"babe listen. this matters. GPT-5.6 dropped in three flavors. Sol, Terra, and Luna. Sol is the smart one. It had to clear government safety evals before release, like getting paroled. Luna is the cheap one. Terra exists.

Grok 4.5 launched the day BEFORE GPT-5.6. On purpose. So it could have 24 hours of being frontier-adjacent. Elon calls it Opus-class, which is a polite way of saying it competes with Anthropic's previous model. But it's $2 per million tokens, so nobody will care.

Speaking of Anthropic. Fable 5 just came back online after the government suspended it for three weeks. The best coding model on earth was literally grounded. It's back now, and the rumored GPT-6 is being retrained specifically to beat it.

Sonnet 5 also shipped last week. Near-Opus performance at $2 per million. It's the new free default. Meaning the free tier is now better than what enterprises paid six figures for in January.

Meituan open sourced LongCat-2.0. That's a 1.6 TRILLION parameter model. MIT licensed. From a Chinese FOOD DELIVERY company. Your DoorDash equivalent is now a frontier lab. Let that one sit.

ByteDance launched Seedream 5 Pro. Almost as good as GPT Image 2 at 4x cheaper, because ByteDance's entire corporate strategy is 'same thing but cheaper.' It beat Meta's new Muse Image, which trains on your public Instagram photos unless you opt out. Your 2014 gym mirror selfie is now foundational research.

OpenAI also shipped GPT-Live, a voice model that goes 'mhmm' and 'got it' WHILE you're still talking. We finally taught AI to pretend to listen. Full parity with husbands achieved.

And Ollama raised $65M so you can run the open source versions of all of this on your own laptop, which is great for privacy and also heating your apartment.

And btw it’s just Thursday"
title_cn:
我向我的妻子解释本周的模特发布情况。

“宝贝听着。这很重要。GPT-5.6 分为三种版本。Sol、Terra 和 Luna。Sol 是聪明的版本。它在发布之前必须通过政府安全评估，比如假释。Luna 是便宜的版本。Terra 存在。

Grok 4.5 在 GPT-5.6 之前发布。故意的。所以它可以有 24 小时处于边境附近。 Elon 将其称为 Opus 级，这是一种礼貌的说法，表示它与 Anthropic 之前的型号竞争。但每百万代币的价格为 2 美元，所以没有人会在意。

说到人择。 《神鬼寓言 5》在政府暂停播放三周后才重新上线。地球上最好的编码模型确实是扎根的。现在它又回来了，传闻中的 GPT-6 正在接受专门的重新训练以击败它。

Sonnet 5 也于上周发货。每百万美元 2 美元的性能接近 Opus。这是新的免费默认设置。这意味着现在的免费套餐比企业在一月份支付六位数的费用要好。

美团开源LongCat-2.0。这是一个 1.6 万亿参数模型。麻省理工学院许可。来自一家中国食品配送公司。 DoorDash 相当于现在的前沿实验室。让那个人坐下。

字节跳动推出Seedream 5 Pro。几乎与 GPT Image 2 一样好，但便宜 4 倍，因为字节跳动的整个公司战略是“相同的东西，但更便宜”。它击败了 Meta 的新 Muse Image，除非你选择退出，否则它会在你的 Instagram 公开照片上进行训练。你 2014 年在健身房对着镜子自拍的照片现已成为基础研究。

OpenAI 还推出了 GPT-Live，这是一种语音模型，当你还在说话时，它会发出“嗯嗯”和“明白了”的声音。我们终于教会了人工智能假装倾听。实现了与丈夫的完全平等。

Ollama 筹集了 6500 万美元，这样你就可以在你自己的笔记本电脑上运行所有这些的开源版本，这对于保护隐私和为你的公寓供暖非常有用。

顺便说一句，今天只是星期四”
