name: Madhu Guru
title:
I’ve had the chance to see this ecosystem from the different sides - enterprises, agent startups, data providers, labs. 

The core tension here is that the business models, moats, value exchange, execution playbooks for all players are being figured out in real time.

This debate around token pricing is really a debate over where value will accrue - models, application layer, distribution or other layers.

Expect a lot of strong opinions from the different sides to shape the narrative in their favor.
title_cn:
我有机会从不同的方面——企业、代理初创公司、数据提供商、实验室——看到这个生态系统。 

这里的核心张力是，所有参与者的商业模式、护城河、价值交换、执行手册都在实时弄清楚。

围绕代币定价的争论实际上是关于价值将在哪里产生的争论——模型、应用程序层、分发或其他层。

期待来自不同方面的大量强烈意见来塑造对他们有利的叙述。

name: Peter Yang
title:
Ok I've fallen for the ragebaiting and have been triggered by this so I'm going to say this: 

Whether intentional or not, there's a lot of virality that comes from:

1. Pick the most ragebait moment of the conversation
2. Make a clip of that exact moment
3. Get tons of retweets and views

I can tell it's rage baiting (just like the Corgi founder clip) but here's the thing - if even one parent watches this clip and decides to neglect their kids more then the damage is done.

And yes it's hard to work at home when your kid comes back at 3 pm (or 1 pm with summer camp!) but we all make our choices.
title_cn:
好吧，我已经陷入了愤怒的漩涡中，并且被这个触发了，所以我要这样说： 

无论有意还是无意，大量的病毒式传播都来自于：

1. 选择谈话中最令人愤怒的时刻
2. 录制那一刻的片段
3.获得大量转发和浏览

我可以看出这是愤怒的诱饵（就像柯基创始人的剪辑一样），但事情是这样的 - 如果即使有一位家长观看了这个剪辑并决定更多地忽视他们的孩子，那么伤害就已经造成了。

是的，当你的孩子下午 3 点回来（夏令营的时候是下午 1 点！），在家工作是很困难的，但我们都会做出自己的选择。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
Almost all AI model and agent progress is downstream from evals. Open weights post training for specific domains comes down to evals. Agent improvements in the applied AI layer is all about evals. Agentic enterprise deployments that actually can augment work is all about evals. It’s all evals.

This will become a core competency of any enterprise in the future. The companies that are able to best understand their own (and/or customers) workflows and how well agents participate in that work will be in the best position to actually drive real automation.
title_cn:
几乎所有人工智能模型和代理的进展都是评估的下游。特定领域训练后的开放权重归结为评估。应用人工智能层中的代理改进全都与评估有关。实际上可以增强工作的代理企业部署都与评估有关。这都是评估。

这将成为未来任何企业的核心能力。能够最好地了解自己（和/或客户）工作流程以及代理参与该工作的程度的公司将处于真正推动真正自动化的最佳位置。

name: Zara Zhang
title:
A good rule of thumb for preventing AI slop (in writing, design, etc):

Is your input (the context) longer than the output? 

I've found that for the AI to produce quality results, my input is often 3-5 times the length of the output.

If your input is much shorter than the output, it's almost certainly going to produce slop
title_cn:
防止人工智能失误（在写作、设计等方面）的一个好的经验法则是：

您的输入（上下文）比输出长吗？ 

我发现，为了让人工智能产生高质量的结果，我的输入通常是输出长度的 3-5 倍。

如果你的输入比输出短得多，几乎肯定会产生溢出

name: Aaron Levie
title:
Another new idea to push the state of AI architectures forward. Sakana released a model that effectively uses a mixture of models to get work done. You get a single API but then the work gets farmed out the model that best performs the task. 

“Fugu manages model selection, delegation, verification, and synthesis automatically. It solves tasks directly when that is enough, or coordinates a team of expert models when a problem calls for more. The complexity of a multi-agent system never reaches your code.”

This is generally how applied AI products are building their agent harnesses at this point, but the idea of making this an LLM that any developer can interact with is also a great idea. As we get more innovation with both frontier closed and OSS models, there’s going to be a ton of value produced for the layer that can route the best.
title_cn:
另一个推动人工智能架构向前发展的新想法。 Sakana 发布了一个模型，该模型有效地使用混合模型来完成工作。您获得一个 API，但随后工作会被外包给最能执行任务的模型。 

“Fugu 自动管理模型选择、委托、验证和综合。当足够时，它会直接解决任务，或者当问题需要更多时协调专家模型团队。多智能体系统的复杂性永远不会达到您的代码。”

这通常是应用人工智能产品目前构建其代理工具的方式，但将其设为任何开发人员都可以与之交互的法学硕士的想法也是一个好主意。随着我们在边界封闭和 OSS 模型方面获得更多创新，将为能够路由最佳路径的层产生大量价值。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
Agents will use software 100X more than people. 

When that happens, theres a huge need for guardrails on what the agents are doing so they don’t leak data or change the wrong information, authoritative sources of truth for them to work with, logging and auditing of what they’re doing, the ability to collaborate with people through these systems, and more.

A simple query on any given agentic task could pull in more data than a user touches in a month. As a result, there are lots of categories of software that when it goes headless that the usage and value go up substantially. Agents will end up using our CRM data, documents and corporate knowledge, analytics data, and other information far more than people ever did.

The platforms that can move toward the model of powering these headless interactions, and have a business model and technology strategy to support this, will be in the best position in the future.
title_cn:
代理使用软件的次数比人多 100 倍。 

当这种情况发生时，就非常需要对特工正在做的事情进行防护，这样他们就不会泄露数据或更改错误信息，需要有权威的事实来源供他们使用，记录和审计他们正在做的事情，通过这些系统与人们协作的能力等等。

对任何给定代理任务的简单查询可能会获取比用户一个月内接触的数据还要多的数据。因此，有很多类别的软件在实现无头后，其使用率和价值都会大幅上升。代理商最终将比人们更多地使用我们的 CRM 数据、文档和公司知识、分析数据和其他信息。

能够走向支持这些无头交互的模式，并拥有支持这一模式的商业模式和技术战略的平台，将在未来处于最佳位置。

name: Peter Yang
title:
Why HTML turned out to be the foundation for agentic video making from @liu8in:

“We’ve been trying to build a video agent. However, we learned the hard way that agents have no visual intelligence.

So that’s when we turned to code. HTML is the LLM’s native language. LLMs can express not only information, but also visual aesthetics through HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

Footage, images, assets, SVGs can all sit on top of HTML."

📌 As an example, see how you can make a product video from any website URL here: https://t.co/Q2p8XzCfc6
title_cn:
@liu8in 为何 HTML 成为代理视频制作的基础：

“我们一直在尝试构建一个视频代理。然而，我们惨痛地认识到代理没有视觉智能。

这就是我们转向代码的时候。 HTML 是法学硕士的母语。 LLM 不仅可以表达信息，还可以通过 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 表达视觉美感。

素材、图像、资产、SVG 都可以位于 HTML 之上。”

📌 作为示例，请参阅如何从此处的任何网站 URL 制作产品视频：https://t.co/Q2p8XzCfc6

name: Aaron Levie
title:
Pretty remarkable what’s happening with open weights AI right now. We’re seeing models achieve SOTA results on specific tasks, and getting close to frontier on some areas of coding and other domains. 

The more that open weights is able to maintain only a marginal gap from the frontier, instead of a widening gap, the more value that can be created with AI.

Incidentally, this is actually fine for the frontier labs as well; if we can lower the cost of an overall task then AI usage goes up in general. You’re still likely using frontier models for planning, orchestration, reviewing, and other parts of work. 

But this is all very good for the applied layer of AI, which is now in a great position to cost optimize workloads with cheaper models or use tailored open models post-trained for specific tasks to improve performance.
title_cn:
目前开放权重人工智能所发生的事情非常引人注目。我们看到模型在特定任务上取得了 SOTA 结果，并且在某些编码领域和其他领域接近前沿。 

开放权重越能保持与前沿的边际差距，而不是差距不断扩大，人工智能可以创造的价值就越大。

顺便说一句，这对于前沿实验室来说实际上也很好。如果我们能够降低整体任务的成本，那么人工智能的使用率总体上会上升。您仍然可能使用前沿模型来进行规划、编排、审查和其他工作部分。 

但这对于人工智能的应用层来说都是非常好的，它现在处于一个有利的位置，可以使用更便宜的模型来优化工作负载的成本，或者使用针对特定任务进行后训练的定制开放模型来提高性能。

name: Nikunj Kothari
title:
The biggest problem with AI is that priors need to be reset every few weeks.. and it seems like most people are incapable of doing that. 

I talk to so many people who say xyz doesn’t work and when I ask when was the last time they tried testing it, the answer is always “a few months ago.” 

Brother that’s like eons ago in AI timelines. This is why each person needs to have their own evals of hard tasks and weekly tinker time to truly understand where the frontier is. 

And then you need to talk to enterprise buyers weekly who are usually two years behind. But they are the buyers so you need to understand how to market. 

The combination of the two will give you a pretty good sense of where to invest time and capital. If you just do these two weekly, you’ll be ahead of 99% of people.
title_cn:
人工智能最大的问题是，先验需要每隔几周重置一次……而且似乎大多数人都无法做到这一点。 

我和很多人交谈过，他们说 xyz 不起作用，当我问他们最后一次尝试测试它是什么时候时，答案总是“几个月前”。 

兄弟，这就像人工智能时间线上的亿万年前一样。这就是为什么每个人都需要对艰巨的任务和每周的修补时间进行自己的评估，以真正了解前沿在哪里。 

然后你需要每周与通常落后两年的企业买家交谈。但他们是买家，所以你需要了解如何营销。 

两者的结合将使您很好地了解在哪里投资时间和资金。如果你每周做这两件事，你就会领先于 99% 的人。

name: Madhu Guru
title:
The Product role is having an identity crisis too.

Engineering has found its AI-native interface - SWE agents dramatically increase individual leverage.
Companies are asking PMs to use AI, but they haven't evolved the role.

So there are two camps.

The old-school PM.
AI accelerates the old job: more PRDs, more strategy decks, more docs. Lots of output, not much judgment.

The Builder PM.
Builder PMs use AI to expand their role across the product lifecycle.

They explore a much larger surface area of ideas to arrive at the best. They run agents for market and user research, query logs and analytics directly, generate competing ideas, then curate the best ones.

They constantly evolve their workflows to take advantage of the latest in AI.

Their outputs are increasingly prototypes rather than docs - engineers react far more constructively to demos than docs.

They do this all without compromising on the craft -they still form a strong point of view on what should be built and why.

I think the role is moving much closer to Builder PMs.
title_cn:
产品角色也面临着身份危机。

Engineering 发现了其 AI 原生界面 - SWE 代理极大地提高了个人影响力。
公司要求产品经理使用人工智能，但他们还没有发展这个角色。

所以有两个阵营。

老派的总理。
人工智能加速了旧的工作：更多的 PRD、更多的策略平台、更多的文档。输出很多，判断不多。

建筑商 PM。
建筑商产品经理使用人工智能来扩展他们在整个产品生命周期中的角色。

他们探索更大范围的想法，以达到最好的效果。他们运行市场和用户研究代理，直接查询日志和分析，产生竞争性想法，然后策划最好的想法。

他们不断改进工作流程，以利用最新的人工智能技术。

他们的输出越来越多地是原型而不是文档——工程师对演示的反应比文档更具建设性。

他们在完成这一切的同时，并没有对工艺做出任何妥协——他们仍然对应该建造什么以及为什么建造形成了强烈的观点。

我认为这个角色越来越接近建筑商产品经理。

name: Peter Yang
title:
I used to be a die-hard Claude Code user.

Codex has won me over because:

→ GPT-5.5 is excellent
→ Fast mode + generous limits = more reps
→ Little touches like steering, auto remote control on phone, etc

But most of all Codex's browser and computer use capabilities are simply goated. I built so many workflows relying on those two things alone instead of hunting for APIs.

I still use Claude Code too. The app seems to be getting better and the design and frontend capability of Opus is still much better than GPT. Whenever Fable comes back that's another reason to go back.

Honestly, I hope these two compete forever and other players (Cursor/Grok, Gemini, etc) all stay competitive.

This way the builder keeps winning 🙂
title_cn:
我曾经是克劳德代码的铁杆用户。

Codex 赢得了我的青睐，因为：

→ GPT-5.5 非常优秀
→ 快速模式 + 慷慨的限制 = 更多次数
→ 一些小细节，如转向、手机自动远程控制等

但最重要的是，Codex 的浏览器和计算机使用功能都非常有限。我仅依靠这两件事构建了如此多的工作流程，而不是寻找 API。

我也仍然使用克劳德代码。该应用程序似乎越来越好，Opus 的设计和前端能力仍然比 GPT 好得多。每当《神鬼寓言》回归时，这就是回去的另一个理由。

老实说，我希望这两个人永远竞争，而其他玩家（Cursor/Grok、Gemini 等）都保持竞争力。

这样建造者就能不断获胜 🙂

name: Guillermo Rauch
title:
The next hot programming language is… markdown.

A minimal eve agent:

📂 𝚊𝚐𝚎𝚗𝚝/
  📄 𝚒𝚗𝚜𝚝𝚛𝚞𝚌𝚝𝚒𝚘𝚗𝚜.𝚖𝚍
  📂 𝚜𝚔𝚒𝚕𝚕𝚜/
     📄 𝚢𝚘𝚞𝚛-𝚎𝚡𝚙𝚎𝚛𝚝𝚒𝚜𝚎.𝚖𝚍

Deployable in one command: 𝚟𝚎𝚛𝚌𝚎𝚕.

It’s the most accessible programming has ever been. And likely will ever be, at least for the generation of software fully defined and controlled by us humans.

(As a fun fact, one of the initial prototypes for eve was codenamed 𝚕𝚊𝚜𝚝 by @timolins, both in homage to ‘@nextjs for agents’ but also in recognition of how enduring eve’s design feels to us.)
title_cn:
下一个热门编程语言是……markdown。

最小的前夕代理：

📂𝚊𝚐𝚎𝚗𝚝/
  📄𝚒𝚗𝚜𝚝𝚛𝚞𝚌𝚝𝚒𝚘𝚗𝚜.𝚖𝚍
  📂𝚜𝚔𝚒𝚕𝚕𝚜/
     📄𝚢𝚘𝚞𝚛-𝚎𝚡𝚙𝚎𝚛𝚝𝚒𝚜𝚎.𝚖𝚍

可通过一个命令进行部署：𝚟𝚎𝚛𝚌𝚎𝚕。

这是有史以来最容易访问的节目。而且很可能永远如此，至少对于完全由我们人类定义和控制的软件的生成而言。

（有趣的是，@timolins 将 eve 的最初原型之一命名为 𝚕𝚊𝚜𝚝，既是为了向“@nextjs for Agents”致敬，也是为了认识到 eve 的设计给我们带来的经久不衰的感觉。）

name: Amanda Askell
title: A counter to this is "yes but people *don't* ignore it". But not ignoring things on scans is our norm because, until recently, we only did scans if there was a clear need. If we move to a scan-more-often paradigm, the norms of what we do with that information will surely adjust.
title_cn: 对此的反驳是“是的，但人们*不会*忽视它”。但不忽视扫描中的内容是我们的常态，因为直到最近，我们只在有明确需要的情况下才进行扫描。如果我们转向更频繁地扫描范式，我们处理这些信息的规范肯定会调整。

name: Amanda Askell
title: The view that we shouldn't do more medical scans because incidental findings cause a lot of harm doesn't sit well with me. It seems like the issue it points to isn't the scan but the response to it. If you see something on a scan but have no other symptoms, you could ignore it.
title_cn: 我们不应该做更多的医学扫描，因为偶然的发现会造成很大的伤害，这种观点并不适合我。它指出的问题似乎不是扫描，而是对其的响应。如果您在扫描中看到某些内容但没有其他症状，则可以忽略它。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
The main variable in getting success with agents is whether you can get the agent the context it needs to do its work; and a major factor in that is if you can create a shared working area for that agent that a human can understand as well. 

This is one of the reasons why agents using file systems is such a big deal. It creates a unified system that both the person and the agent can work within to pass around data. 

“What they need is a working set: plans, notes, task lists, policies, drafts, summaries, logs, corrections,  decisions, etc. For that layer, a filesystem-shaped interface tends to be more legible to both the model and the humans supervising it.”

It turns out giving agents access to the systems we already know how to use, but in a way that is best optimized for them, is the perfect primitive for agents to work.
title_cn:
代理取得成功的主要变量是您能否为代理提供完成其工作所需的上下文；其中一个主要因素是您是否可以为该代理创建一个人类也可以理解的共享工作区域。 

这就是使用文件系统的代理如此重要的原因之一。它创建了一个统一的系统，人员和代理都可以在其中工作以传递数据。 

“他们需要的是一个工作集：计划、笔记、任务列表、政策、草稿、摘要、日志、更正、决策等。对于该层，文件系统形状的界面对于模型和监督它的人员来说往往更清晰。”

事实证明，让代理能够访问我们已经知道如何使用的系统，但以最适合他们的方式进行访问，是代理工作的完美原语。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
This is a good update for getting access to Fable. It also gives us a view into what the future is likely going to look like with AI regulation. 

The government will have frameworks that are used to determine future model releases past a certain threshold of capability or compute levels. Given all the constituents involved, and the economic and societal significance of AI, this was practically an inevitability.

It may seem small but the implications are massive. It will mean that each model update will go through an extensive review, testing, and feedback process. And in that processes lots of groups will weigh in on the risk of the model, and there will be lots of subjectivity on what the actual risks are or practicalities of exploiting those risks. 

A positive potential future here would be we still get massive model progress but they just happen in bigger jumps at once, where the labs pack in major improvements since the cost and slow down of each review stacks up. 

On the other side, the risk is that past a certain threshold we may not get to see the rapid back and forth of model progress that we’ve gotten used to which can have negative compounding effects. Hoping for the former outcome.
title_cn:
这是访问《神鬼寓言》的一个很好的更新。它还让我们了解人工智能监管的未来可能会是什么样子。 

政府将制定框架，用于确定超过特定能力或计算水平阈值的未来模型版本。考虑到所涉及的所有组成部分以及人工智能的经济和社会意义，这实际上是不可避免的。

它可能看起来很小，但影响却是巨大的。这意味着每个模型更新都将经过广泛的审查、测试和反馈过程。在这个过程中，许多团体都会权衡模型的风险，并且对于实际风险是什么或利用这些风险的实用性会有很多主观性。 

一个积极的潜在未来是，我们仍然会取得巨大的模型进展，但它们只是一次性发生更大的跳跃，实验室会进行重大改进，因为每次审查的成本和速度都会增加。 

另一方面，风险在于，超过某个阈值后，我们可能无法看到我们已经习惯的模型进展的快速来回，这可能会产生负面的复合效应。期待之前的结果。

name: Nan Yu
title:
It took us a lot of iterations to find the right pattern for this. Early on we were very naive and we thought that the agent could one shot project updates without much input from users.

That approach ended up creating a bunch of slop, because users disengaged their brain from the process of writing an update.

By switching to a multi turn interactive mode, the agent prompts you and asks you what to emphasize, what's most important, what context is missing, etc.

This simple change elicits much better project updates and ensures that the user has a chance to steer rather than simply being asked to say yes to everything.

A very good example of introducing friction for a net benefit.
title_cn:
我们经过多次迭代才找到合适的模式。早期我们非常天真，我们认为代理可以在没有用户太多输入的情况下一次性完成项目更新。

这种方法最终产生了一堆废话，因为用户将他们的大脑从编写更新的过程中脱离出来。

通过切换到多轮交互模式，代理会提示您并询问您要强调什么、什么是最重要的、缺少什么上下文等。

这个简单的更改引发了更好的项目更新，并确保用户有机会引导，而不是简单地被要求对所有事情说“是”。

这是引入摩擦以获得净收益的一个很好的例子。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
The past couple months we may be witnessing what the Applied AI layer will look like at scale. Despite some of the initial critique that this would just be a thin layer on the LLM, it’s turning out that actually driving agentic workflows in an enterprise is far more complex. And anywhere there’s complexity you generally gain a moat and value over time.

Here are a few of the components that appear to make up the playbook based on the examples we’re collectively seeing in coding, legal, healthcare, customer support, financial services and other fields:

* Build the features that bridge the gap between the intelligence and the workflow. Some workflows can be automated by simply going to a general purpose interface, but others need tuned interfaces and features tied to the work they’re augmenting or automating. They need features that are specific to capturing the kind of data that’s needed as context for the agent. And they need a variety of bespoke tools for the agent to use, and unique interfaces for the human-in-the-loop UX. Going far deeper than just presenting the output tokens is clearly critical, and the more depth there is here definitionally the more sustaining value.

* Act as the model router balancing frontier intelligence with cheaper models. A natural advantage that any model neutral platform has is that it can naturally (in a business model-aligned way) leverage whatever level of intelligence is necessary for the workflows they’re automating to get done. There are plenty of scenarios where you need GPT-5.5 or Fable level capability, and also lots of workloads where a more efficient closed or open weights do the trick. Only the companies that have deep evals on specific tasks across all models, and the ability business model wise to leverage them, are in a great position.

* Drive the actual implementation and change management via FDE or equivalent. A big reason the applied layer works at scale is that most enterprises need some degree of help and support with change management in implementing agents for their workflows. Data has to be cleaned up and moved to modern systems, processes have to be re-engineered and documented, workflows have to be evaled, SLAs have to get achieved, and so on. All of this is going to be unique for every type of process that gets implemented, which means the companies that have expertise in a given domain and come with all the relevant best practices will be in a strong position.

* Implement domain specific GTM that creates expertise in that field. Beyond FDEs the companies that can build sales and GTM motions aligned to their domains also have a natural advantage. Most IT and line of business leaders have too many things to do in any given day; so if you’re not on their agenda, likely someone else is. Depending on the industry, there are entirely different sets of language you use, ways of working through security and compliance, regulatory controls you have to support, industry events that companies convene at, different system integrator and consulting partners you need to work with, and so on. The more generalized this gets the less you can speak the customers language, which is where the applied layer has a leg up.

A final note. There remains a view that a lot of this is all mitigated by model intelligence alone, and the bitter lesson solves all of this in the limit. That’s possibly true, but enterprises need help changing *today*. And many aspects of how to bring intelligence to real world work don’t only depend on the axis of the pure capability of the model, so most of what you’re doing now to win ends up being important no matter how good the models get.
title_cn:
在过去的几个月里，我们可能会见证应用人工智能层的大规模发展。尽管一些最初的批评认为这只是法学硕士的一个薄层，但事实证明，在企业中实际驱动代理工作流程要复杂得多。任何有复杂性的地方，随着时间的推移，你通常都会获得护城河和价值。

以下是根据我们在编码、法律、医疗保健、客户支持、金融服务和其他领域共同看到的示例组成的剧本的一些组件：

* 构建弥合智能和工作流程之间差距的功能。有些工作流程可以通过简单地进入通用界面来实现自动化，但其他工作流程则需要与他们正在增强或自动化的工作相关的调整界面和功能。他们需要特定于捕获代理上下文所需的数据类型的功能。他们需要各种定制工具供代理使用，以及用于人机交互用户体验的独特界面。显然，比仅仅呈现输出代币更深入的内容至关重要，而且深度越深，定义上的持续价值就越大。

* 充当平衡前沿智能与更便宜型号的模型路由器。任何模型中立平台所具有的一个天然优势是，它可以自然地（以与业务模型一致的方式）利用其自动化完成的工作流程所需的任何级别的智能。在很多场景中，您都需要 GPT-5.5 或 Fable 级别的功能，而且在很多工作负载中，更高效的封闭式或开放式权重可以发挥作用。只有那些对所有模型的特定任务进行深入评估，并且有能力利用商业模型明智地利用这些任务的公司，才能处于有利地位。

* 通过 FDE 或同等方式推动实际实施和变更管理。应用层大规模工作的一个重要原因是，大多数企业在为其工作流程实施代理时需要一定程度的变更管理帮助和支持。数据必须清理并转移到现代系统，流程必须重新设计和记录，工作流程必须评估，SLA 必须实现，等等。所有这些对于所实施的每种类型的流程来说都是独一无二的，这意味着在特定领域拥有专业知识并拥有所有相关最佳实践的公司将处于有利地位。

* 实施特定领域的 GTM，以创造该领域的专业知识。除了 FDE 之外，能够建立与其领域相符的销售和 GTM 动议的公司也具有天然优势。大多数 IT 和业务线领导者每天都有太多的事情要做；因此，如果你不在他们的议程上，很可能其他人也在。根据行业的不同，您使用的语言、安全性和合规性的工作方式、您必须支持的监管控制、公司召开的行业活动、您需要合作的不同系统集成商和咨询合作伙伴等等都存在完全不同的语言集。越通用，你能说客户语言的就越少，而这正是应用层的优势所在。

最后一点。人们仍然认为，仅靠模型智能就可以缓解很多问题，而惨痛的教训在极限内解决了所有这些问题。这可能是真的，但企业*今天*需要帮助改变。如何将智能带入现实世界工作的许多方面不仅仅取决于模型的纯粹能力轴，因此无论模型变得多么好，你现在为获胜所做的大部分事情最终都是重要的。

name: Zara Zhang
title:
Don't use AI for writing until you develop your own taste and voice

Using AI to write isn't inherently bad. The danger is using AI to write before you've developed your taste for what is good content. If the AI produces slop, you won't even recognize it as slop

Read a lot to figure out what good looks like. Write a lot to know what your voice sounds like. Only then, use AI to help you write, but make sure it actually sounds like you
title_cn:
在你形成自己的品味和声音之前，不要使用人工智能进行写作

使用人工智能写作本质上并不是坏事。危险在于，在你对好内容形成自己的品味之前就使用人工智能进行写作。如果人工智能产生了溢出物，你甚至不会识别出它是溢出物

多读书才能明白什么是好的。写很多东西来了解你的声音是什么样的。然后，使用人工智能来帮助你写作，但要确保它听起来确实像你

name: Garry Tan
title:
Rough estimate on $ productivity lost by Fable 5 ban: $12M per hour

Frontier AI-coding daily actives, mid-2026: 5M devs 
Fully-loaded cost: $90/hr 

Work routed to Fable in 48 hours: 17.8%
Fable is on average ~15% more productive 

Effective throughput loss per dev = 17.8% × 15% ≈ 2.7% of output

2.7% × $90/hr = ~$2.40/dev/hr × 5M devs = $12M per working hour
title_cn:
粗略估计《神鬼寓言 5》禁令造成的生产力损失：每小时 1200 万美元

Frontier AI 编码每日活跃量，2026 年中期：500 万开发人员 
满载成本：90 美元/小时 

48 小时内发送至《神鬼寓言》的工作：17.8%
《神鬼寓言》的平均生产力提高约 15% 

每个开发的有效吞吐量损失 = 17.8% × 15% ≈ 输出的 2.7%

2.7% × 90 美元/小时 = ~2.40 美元/开发人员/小时 × 500 万开发人员 = 每个工作小时 1200 万美元

name: Zara Zhang
title:
The thing about vibe coded personal apps:

Building the thing takes a day. Finding out if you'll actually use it takes a week. Most of my dead projects worked fine. I just never opened them.

Most products are built for a person who doesn't exist. Someone who remembers to open the app, clicks the right button, does step 1, 2, 3 every day. Real humans are lazy & forgetful. Build for that person instead
title_cn:
关于振动编码个人应用程序的事情：

建造这个东西需要一天的时间。确定您是否会真正使用它需要一周的时间。我的大多数失败项目都运行良好。我只是从来没有打开过它们。

大多数产品都是为不存在的人打造的。记得打开应用程序、点击右侧按钮、每天执行步骤 1、2、3 的人。真正的人类是懒惰且健忘的。为那个人构建

name: Garry Tan
title:
Which leads to… Bowen’s differentiation of self

Low differentiation = highly reactive to others' emotional cues, loses self in relationships, can't hold boundaries under pressure — collapses into "we-ness" (everyone must feel the same or the bond feels threatened).

Two flavors of the same disease: chameleons (adjust themselves to please, fusion mode) and bullies (force others to conform, the asshole mode). Both are undifferentiated. The self-abandoner and the asshole are the same person.

The fix is the same: lower the chronic anxiety, raise the capacity to stay a separate self in contact.
title_cn:
这导致了……鲍文的自我分化

低分化=对他人的情绪暗示高度反应，在人际关系中迷失自我，在压力下无法保持界限——崩溃到“我们”（每个人都必须有同样的感觉，否则关系就会受到威胁）。

同一种疾病的两种形式：变色龙（调整自己以取悦他人，融合模式）和恶霸（强迫他人服从，混蛋模式）。两者无差别。自暴自弃者和混蛋是同一个人。

解决方法是一样的：降低慢性焦虑，提高保持独立自我接触的能力。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
One of the biggest questions in AI is how far behind open weights models remain from closed models at any given time. There are huge differences in market structures depending on whether open weights models remain 3 or 6 months behind, or if they fall behind by years. 

The answer to this will determine how the chip stack plays out, where inference can be run, what sovereign AI looks like, what happens at the applied AI layer, what the margin structure looks like in AI, how much companies can afford to spend on AI, and more.

At the moment the open weights players appear to be holding up at keeping close to frontier levels of capability. Will be fun to see how this plays out.
title_cn:
人工智能中最大的问题之一是，在任何给定时间，开放权重模型与封闭模型的差距有多大。市场结构存在巨大差异，具体取决于开放权重模型是否落后 3 个月或 6 个月，或者是否落后数年。 

这个问题的答案将决定芯片堆栈如何发挥作用、推理可以在哪里运行、主权人工智能是什么样子、应用人工智能层会发生什么、人工智能的利润结构是什么样子、公司可以在人工智能上花多少钱等等。

目前，公开重量级选手似乎仍在保持接近前沿的能力水平。看看结果如何会很有趣。

name: Madhu Guru
title:
The real prize in the SpaceX-Cursor deal is the agentic harness that will become the core for automating all knowledge work at scale.

Here’s what SpaceX is getting:

1. Production-grade agentic harness  -planning, context management, tool use, iteration, verification, memory, error recovery. 
Any product experience can be completely redesigned in an AI-native way with this harness.

2. Expertise on the full AI stack - model, evals, harness, application layer.

3. End to end product lifecycle focus: product strategy -> user journeys -> GTM. Everything optimized for one job: helping software engineers build.

Very few companies do even one of these well. Cursor brings all three.
title_cn:
SpaceX 与 Cursor 交易的真正价值是代理工具，它将成为大规模自动化所有知识工作的核心。

以下是 SpaceX 得到的结果：

1. 生产级代理工具——规划、上下文管理、工具使用、迭代、验证、内存、错误恢复。 
任何产品体验都可以通过该工具以人工智能原生方式完全重新设计。

2. 完整人工智能堆栈的专业知识 - 模型、评估、利用、应用层。

3. 端到端产品生命周期重点：产品策略->用户旅程->GTM。一切都针对一项工作进行了优化：帮助软件工程师进行构建。

很少有公司能做到其中一项。光标带来了这三个。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
The Cursor deal is symbolically quite significant. It was effectively the first mega success in the applied layer of AI. 

They firmly proved out the value proposition of having a deep domain focus, the role you play as a model router, when to lean into frontier models vs. when to train your own, and the role of applied AI GTM and distribution to make sure you’re actually taking advantage of the market opportunity. 

Every aspect of their business was tuned to carve out ground and keep doubling down in a highly competitive space. This is really the first at scale template for how to execute this playbook.
title_cn:
Cursor 交易具有相当重要的象征意义。这实际上是人工智能应用层的第一次巨大成功。 

他们坚定地证明了深耕领域的价值主张、模型路由器的角色、何时倾向于前沿模型与何时训练自己的模型，以及应用 AI GTM 和分发的作用，以确保你真正利用市场机会。 

他们业务的各个方面都进行了调整，以便在竞争激烈的领域中开拓出一片天地并不断加倍努力。这确实是第一个关于如何执行此剧本的大规模模板。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
Key post that gives a bit of insight into what the future of AI could look like.

“The most interesting thing happening in AI isn't that one model is getting smarter. It's that intelligence is becoming increasingly customizable. The companies that win won't necessarily be the ones with the biggest models. They'll be the ones that turn intelligence into something uniquely their own.”

The ability to combine your unique data, workflows, and a layer that can route intelligence to whatever model best performs the task is clearly the future.
title_cn:
这篇重要文章让我们深入了解人工智能的未来。

“人工智能领域发生的最有趣的事情不是一种模型变得越来越智能。而是智能变得越来越可定制。获胜的公司不一定是那些拥有最大模型的公司。他们将是那些将智能变成自己独特的东西的公司。”

将独特的数据、工作流程和一个可以将智能路由到最能执行任务的模型的层相结合的能力显然是未来的趋势。

name: Nikunj Kothari
title:
I now know 32 VCs who have moved back to operating in the last 12 months.. everyone from associates to GPs. 

Maybe it’s this way all the time and I’m just noticing it, but the pace seems to be accelerating. And they seem much much happier? 

From a professional perspective, it makes sense especially if you are junior. You get to work directly with customers and work as a team. A lot more autonomy than having to pander to GPs. 

From a compensation perspective too, why wait for carry for 13 years when you can have a chance of liquidity a lot sooner. 

As someone who had been operating for a while but loving venture rn, I completely get the trade. To the remaining players, see you on the field 🫡
title_cn:
我现在认识 32 名风险投资家，他们在过去 12 个月内重新开始运营……每个人都从合伙人到全科医生。 

也许一直都是这样，只是我注意到了，但步伐似乎在加快。他们看起来快乐多了？ 

从专业角度来看，这是有道理的，尤其是如果你是初级的。您可以直接与客户合作并作为一个团队工作。比必须迎合全科医生有更多的自主权。 

同样从补偿的角度来看，既然你可以更快地获得流动性，为什么还要等待 13 年呢？ 

作为一个已经经营了一段时间但热爱风险投资的人，我完全理解这个交易。剩下的选手们，场上见🫡

name: Aaron Levie
title:
It’s very easy to say “we need an FDA for AI” or some equivalent government agency. Well this is what that would look like.

The capabilities of AI models have near infinite permutations. It’s going to be very hard have a purely objective set of metrics that can be universally applied before every model release, without extended back and forth, research, and debate between model labs, academics, and the government. 

Now, imagine this same process with every country that you’re doing business with, globally, for every single model release. Add in a backlog of dozens of AI model releases, and you can quickly see how in the limit this will dramatically slow down all AI progress. 

This is why we need to primarily focus on regulating the applied uses of AI, where the risk actually shows up.
title_cn:
很容易说“我们需要一个针对人工智能的 FDA”或一些类似的政府机构。嗯，这就是看起来的样子。

人工智能模型的功能具有近乎无限的排列。如果模型实验室、学术界和政府之间没有长期的来回、研究和辩论，那么在每次模型发布之前都很难拥有一套可以普遍应用的纯粹客观的指标。 

现在，想象一下在全球范围内与您开展业务的每个国家/地区的每个模型发布时都采用相同的流程。再加上数十个 AI 模型版本的积压，您可以很快看到这将如何在有限的情况下极大地减慢所有 AI 的进展。 

这就是为什么我们需要主要关注监管人工智能的应用，因为风险实际上出现在了这一领域。

name: Guillermo Rauch
title:
One of our most requested features, longer Vercel function runtime, is here.

What looks like an innocent tweak of a constant… is actually the conclusion a multi-year compute platform investment.

Builds, Sandbox, and now Functions run on our homegrown microVM-based Fluid compute infrastructure.

This investment has enabled innovations like function multi-concurrency, Active CPU pricing, and Secure Compute for private connectivity to existing cloud workloads… with lots more to come, in fact, in the coming days and weeks 😎
title_cn:
我们最需要的功能之一是更长的 Vercel 函数运行时间。

看起来像是对常数的无害调整……实际上是多年计算平台投资的结论。

构建、沙盒以及现在的函数都在我们自主开发的基于 microVM 的 Fluid 计算基础设施上运行。

这项投资实现了功能多并发、主动 CPU 定价和安全计算等创新，以实现与现有云工作负载的私有连接……事实上，在未来几天和几周内还会有更多创新 😎

havent seen many people outside anthropic ultracode yet. this thing is scarily good at burning tokens but you need to set up your repo to parallelize properly to make use of the fanout that i think subagents are best at.

basically the idea is "subroutines but intelligent". when you undersatnd just how much knowledge work is just yakshaves after yakshaves that require some judgment and intelligence, you start to appreciate that dynamic workflows are not just for coding tasks...

还没见过人类超码之外的很多人。这个东西非常擅长燃烧代币，但你需要设置你的存储库以正确并行化，以利用我认为子代理最擅长的扇出。

基本上这个想法是“子程序但智能”。当您了解有多少知识工作只是一个又一个需要一定判断力和智力的知识工作时，您就会开始意识到动态工作流程不仅仅适用于编码任务......

name: Aaron Levie
title:
Great post. The companies that are able to get their unique IP, institutional knowledge, and data into a format and architecture that lets them capture all of the gains and progress in AI are going to be in the best position in the future.

“the real opportunity is not in picking the best model but instead in building a learning loop on top of models where human capital and token capital compound. You can offload a task, or even a job, but you can never offload your learning. The future of the firm is the ability to compound that learning across people and AI.

This requires a new architectural approach where every business is able to build agentic systems that improve over time, while still retaining control over their IP. A company should be able to switch out a “generalist” model without losing the “company veteran” expertise built into their learning system.”

We’re all collectively figuring out the right architecture for the future of AI. But it’s clear that so much of the power and value will accrue to wherever can best leverage any AI system against their information. This is also why the applied AI layer will also gain so much value over the coming years.
title_cn:
很棒的帖子。那些能够将其独特的知识产权、机构知识和数据转化为一种格式和架构的公司，能够让他们捕获人工智能领域的所有收益和进步，将在未来处于最佳地位。

“真正的机会不在于选择最好的模型，而在于在人力资本和代币资本复合的模型之上建立一个学习循环。你可以卸载一项任务，甚至一份工作，但你永远无法卸载你的学习。公司的未来是能够在人和人工智能之间复合学习的能力。

这需要一种新的架构方法，每个企业都能够构建随着时间的推移而改进的代理系统，同时仍然保留对其 IP 的控制。公司应该能够转变“通才”模式，而不会失去学习系统中内置的“公司资深”专业知识。”

我们正在共同寻找适合人工智能未来的架构。但很明显，只要能够最好地利用人工智能系统的信息，就会产生如此多的力量和价值。这也是为什么应用人工智能层在未来几年也将获得如此大的价值。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
The big winner in all of this is going to be open weights models. This is a huge win for the field, as a risk that was entirely theoretical and untested 2 days ago (that a model could be pulled back), now has a new precedent that’s been set.

The game theory the US should highly consider, and the risk with regulating AI at the model layer vs. applied layer, is that other countries now have even more incentive to develop sovereign AI.

If at any moment a model can be become unavailable to your country’s users or businesses, this poses very real risk on relying on technology from a particular country.

As a result, it forces major countries to charter their own path on AI development, which reduces America’s leadership role in this tech stack over time. The most likely solution that other countries will rely on is open weights models, which currently is generally not coming from the US.

America should be considering all of these downstream implications as it decides how and where in the stack to be regulating AI. At the same time, we should be doing a ton more OSS innovation.
title_cn:
所有这一切的最大赢家将是开放重量模型。这对于该领域来说是一个巨大的胜利，因为两天前完全是理论上且未经测试的风险（模型可能会被撤回）现在已经有了一个新的先例。

美国应该高度考虑的博弈论，以及在模型层与应用层监管人工智能的风险是，其他国家现在更有动力发展主权人工智能。

如果您所在国家/地区的用户或企业在任何时候都无法使用某个模型，那么依赖特定国家/地区的技术就会带来非常现实的风险。

结果，它迫使主要国家制定自己的人工智能发展道路，随着时间的推移，这会削弱美国在这一技术堆栈中的领导作用。其他国家最有可能依赖的解决方案是开放权重模型，目前该模型通常不是来自美国。

美国在决定如何以及在何处监管人工智能时应该考虑所有这些下游影响。与此同时，我们应该进行更多的开源软件创新。

name: Aditya Agarwal
title:
I have been thinking about this photo a lot.

It's a beautiful testament to human civilization and our ability to reach for the stars.

But the thing that I can't get over is that a *human being* took this photo. From a tin cylinder flying on the other side of the moon. 

A machine could have taken it and it would have still been cool. But man was there. And that makes it so much more evocative.
title_cn:
我对这张照片思考了很多。

这是人类文明和我们摘星能力的美丽证明。

但令我无法释怀的是这张照片是一个“人类”拍的。来自在月球另一侧飞行的锡圆筒。 

一台机器可以接受它，但它仍然很酷。但人就在那里。这使得它更加令人回味。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
The layer that can route to the best AI model for the particular job is going to increase in value substantially. There are at least 3 big reasons: 

* Cost optimization: there are plenty of use cases where you need frontier intelligence for some tasks and something far cheaper for others. Even in the same task you may use frontier intelligence for planning and review of the work, but an OSS or cheaper model for the bulk of the workload. This is going to be standard across large buckets of work going forward. 

* Capability maximization: despite the bitter lesson and models generally getting better in the same direction, there are still lots of differences between models. Some are better at tool use, others better at coding, and others again better at certain domains of knowledge work. The ability to route between these at different times is a huge advantage.

* Risk mitigation: while the Fable situation is somewhat of a black swan, it’s possible we’re heading toward a regulatory environment where governments may restrict models at different times based on their approval mechanisms or new things they discover. This means you’re going to want flexibility in being able to deploy workloads across different providers as a form of risk mitigation.

Ultimately, it’s going to increasingly be a a strategic advantage for the applied AI layer that they can effectively route between models. Will be very interesting to see how this evolves.
title_cn:
可以为特定工作路由到最佳人工智能模型的层的价值将大幅增加。至少有3个重要原因： 

* 成本优化：在很多用例中，您需要前沿智能来完成某些任务，而其他任务则需要更便宜的东西。即使在同一任务中，您也可以使用前沿智能来规划和审查工作，但可以使用 OSS 或更便宜的模型来处理大部分工作负载。这将成为未来大量工作的标准。 

* 能力最大化：尽管有惨痛的教训，并且模型通常在同一方向上变得更好，但模型之间仍然存在很多差异。有些人更擅长工具使用，另一些人更擅长编码，而另一些人则更擅长某些知识工作领域。在不同时间在这些之间进行路由的能力是一个巨大的优势。

* 风险缓解：虽然《神鬼寓言》的情况有点像黑天鹅，但我们可能正在走向一个监管环境，政府可能会根据其审批机制或他们发现的新事物在不同时间限制模型。这意味着您需要能够灵活地跨不同提供商部署工作负载，作为降低风险的一种形式。

最终，它们可以在模型之间有效地进行路由，这将越来越成为应用人工智能层的战略优势。看看这是如何演变的将会非常有趣。

name: Madhu Guru
title:
Having been through many frontier model launch reviews, I have empathy for everyone involved.

Launching an LLM isn't like shipping traditional software - you're making a decision about a black box with effectively infinite use cases and infinite failure modes.

The tradeoffs are hard - every increase in capability expands the space of both valuable use cases and potential misuse.

As a lab, you build extensive evals, you red-team, you iterate on the model. You debate tradeoffs across candidate checkpoints before choosing the best one to launch.

Then early-access partners still uncover behaviors you didn't anticipate.

You can never be 100% certain you've understood a frontier model. You focus on reducing the uncertainty enough to launch.

As frontier models become smarter across the industry, that decision will get harder - for labs and regulators.
title_cn:
在经历了许多前沿模型发布评论后，我对所有参与其中的人都有同理心。

启动法学硕士与交付传统软件不同——您正在对一个具有无限用例和无限故障模式的黑匣子做出决定。

权衡是困难的——每一次能力的提高都会扩大有价值的用例和潜在滥用的空间。

作为实验室，您构建广泛的评估、红队、迭代模型。在选择最佳的启动方案之前，您需要对候选检查点之间的权衡进行辩论。

然后，抢先体验的合作伙伴仍然会发现您没有预料到的行为。

您永远无法 100% 确定您已理解前沿模型。您专注于减少足以启动的不确定性。

随着整个行业的前沿模型变得更加智能，对于实验室和监管机构来说，做出决定将变得更加困难。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
This whole Fable export control situation is actually net positive to regulation discourse. It’s an early peek into what AI regulation would end up looking like at scale when enacted at the model layer instead of the specific application of the AI.

The government would have sole discretion over when a model can be released to the to public, based on a bunch of factors that they inherently control. 

In this case, based on the available reporting, the risk is that the model can be jailbroken to deliver increased cyber exploit capabilities. The issue is that actually you want models to be able to have those capabilities on the defense side of cyber as well, and for all intents and purposes, by Anthropic’s own response, you can execute these capabilities today in other models. 

So thus the whole challenge will be that you’re debating with the government, over months and months, with every model release, what these models are actually capable of and what their risks are. Inherently, there’s not only a lot of subjectivity in determining those risks, but there’s also many other factors that go into the risks being practical in the first place.

The net result is that we would end up with backlog of AI releases, progress in the market inherently would dramatically slow down, and AI would start to look more like any other sclerotic industry. If this paradigm had existed 3 years ago at the start of the current AI wave, we’d likely currently be stuck on GPT-4 level intelligence at this point.

This is why, wherever possible, we should be regulating the applied use of AI. We should continue to study and enforce the dangerous use of AI in cyber attacks, financial services risks, fraud, biowarfare, and other spaces. AI safety is incredibly important, but slowing down progress this early in the development of AI I suspect is net harmful.
title_cn:
这整个寓言出口管制形势实际上对监管话语是积极的。这是对人工智能监管在模型层而不是人工智能的具体应用上制定时最终会是什么样子的初步了解。

政府将根据其固有控制的一系列因素，自行决定何时向公众发布模型。 

在这种情况下，根据现有的报告，风险在于该模型可能会被越狱以提供增强的网络利用能力。问题是，实际上您希望模型也能够在网络防御方面拥有这些功能，并且出于所有意图和目的，根据 Anthropic 自己的回应，您现在可以在其他模型中执行这些功能。 

因此，整个挑战将是你与政府就每个模型的发布进行数月的辩论，这些模型的实际能力是什么以及它们的风险是什么。从本质上讲，确定这些风险不仅存在很大的主观性，而且首先还有许多其他因素影响这些风险的实际性。

最终结果是，我们最终会出现人工智能版本积压的情况，市场的进步本质上会急剧放缓，人工智能将开始看起来更像任何其他僵化的行业。如果这种范式在 3 年前当前人工智能浪潮开始时就已经存在，那么我们目前可能会陷入 GPT-4 级别的智能。

这就是为什么我们应该尽可能规范人工智能的应用。我们应该继续研究和加强人工智能在网络攻击、金融服务风险、欺诈、生物战和其他领域的危险使用。人工智能安全非常重要，但在人工智能发展的早期放慢进度，我怀疑是有害的。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
This is a big turning point for AI regulation.

The government is starting to deem some models too powerful for certain uses, which creates a precedent for a range of possible controls in the future.

I’m in the camp that this is unnecessary and we should be primarily regulating the use of AI, as opposed to the underlying models. But, equally, there are plenty of people that actually prefer this outcome.

Either way, it’s unlikely that we’re going back to a world where the government doesn’t have far more meaningful involvement in the rate of AI progress.
title_cn:
这是人工智能监管的一个重大转折点。

政府开始认为某些模型对于某些用途而言过于强大，这为未来一系列可能的控制措施创造了先例。

我的观点是，这是不必要的，我们应该主要规范人工智能的使用，而不是底层模型。但同样，也有很多人实际上更喜欢这种结果。

不管怎样，我们不太可能回到一个政府对人工智能进步速度没有更有意义的参与的世界。

name: Swyx
title:
## The Future Codebase

After the PR dies, after the Code Review dies,
i am seriously wondering if Git needs to die next.

roughly 20-40% of code spend is just managing and updating merge conflicts. necessary evil? or legacy "horseless carriage"? cargo culting the past?

we don't do line by line merge conflicts when we collaborate with human colleagues - instead we chat, suggest edits, do side comments, and an owner ships it. 

btw we also don't do CI/CD even collaborating on documents with serious legal/financial implications.

maybe the future codebase looks more like a Notion or Linear database than .git objects. It will be less efficient, but more scalable. exactly the Salty Lesson.
title_cn:
## 未来的代码库

PR 死掉之后，Code Review 死掉之后，
我真的很想知道 Git 接下来是否需要死掉。

大约 20-40% 的代码支出只是用于管理和更新合并冲突。必要的邪恶？还是传统的“无马马车”？货崇拜过去了？

当我们与人类同事合作时，我们不会逐行合并冲突 - 相反，我们聊天，建议编辑，进行旁注，然后所有者发布它。 

顺便说一句，我们也不进行 CI/CD，甚至不合作处理具有严重法律/财务影响的文档。

也许未来的代码库看起来更像是概念或线性数据库，而不是 .git 对象。它的效率会降低，但可扩展性更高。正是咸教训。

name: Nikunj Kothari
title:
Anyone else have two voices?

I often have two voices that come out both in my writing and how I speak. 

One is the frenetic, time is the enemy, direct, punchy gets to the point quickly and then the second is more calm, measured, structured inquisitive, trying to peel the layers. 

They are distinctive enough that people think it’s two different people (especially in writing). 

They are usually separate but I feel I often oscillate between the two in the same conversation.
title_cn:
还有人有两种声音吗？

我的写作和说话方式中经常会出现两种声音。 

一种是狂热，时间是敌人，直接、有力地快速切入主题，而第二种则更加冷静、谨慎、有条理、好奇，试图剥离层层。 

他们非常独特，以至于人们认为这是两个不同的人（尤其是在写作方面）。 

他们通常是分开的，但我觉得我经常在同一次谈话中在两者之间摇摆不定。

稀有结果的一般人群基本比率要糟糕得多，比两位数百分比更接近于零。 12.3% 的选择机制非常有效。

name: Swyx
title:
## On Loopcraft

One might argue the entire game of the next century is to be able to stack loops as effectively as possible. 

In the early days of each phase, it will be valuable to know when to go **DOWN** a loop when things go wrong (for reliability)… 

but it will probably be more valuable to know how to go **UP** a loop as models improve (for leverage).

If you don’t figure out how to do this, don’t be salty when you lose to those that do.
title_cn:
## 关于 Loopcraft

有人可能会说，下个世纪的整个游戏就是能够尽可能有效地堆叠循环。 

在每个阶段的早期，知道出现问题时何时**向下**循环是很有价值的（为了可靠性）...... 

但随着模型的改进（为了杠杆作用），了解如何进入 **UP** 循环可能更有价值。

如果你不知道如何做到这一点，那么当你输给那些知道如何做到这一点的人时，不要生气。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
At Box, we just surveyed 1,640 IT leaders across the US, Japan, and Europe about agentic AI adoption. Many standout findings, but a big one was that the companies that adopted AI the most are planning to grow headcount the most.

Obviously lots of ways you can read that data and variables mixed in, but it’s actually quite intuitive that the companies that become most productive want to (and are able to) reinvest back into the business to keep getting the gains going. 

The narrative of jobs being wiped out assumes that companies will take a fixed approach to what they want to be able for work on. What’s happening in practice is it’s causing companies to want to light up more engineering projects, sell to more customers, automate more processes to give time back, and more. That all leads to more work to be done by people.
title_cn:
在 Box，我们刚刚对美国、日本和欧洲的 1,640 名 IT 领导者进行了有关代理 AI 采用情况的调查。许多引人注目的发现，但其中最重要的一点是，采用人工智能最多的公司正计划最多地增加员工人数。

显然，您可以通过多种方式读取混合的数据和变量，但实际上非常直观的是，生产力最高的公司希望（并且能够）对业务进行再投资，以继续获得收益。 

关于工作岗位被消灭的说法假设公司将采取固定的方法来处理他们想要的工作。实践中发生的情况是，它导致公司想要启动更多的工程项目、向更多的客户销售产品、自动化更多的流程以节省时间等等。这一切都导致人们需要完成更多的工作。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
Lots of evidence of huge jumps in capability for Fable across coding (and related) tasks. It’s also a major jump in accuracy and success in complex knowledge work tasks.

In our Box AI Complex Work Eval, we tested the model against Opus 4.8 and saw huge boosts across almost every industry. For our eval we give the Box AI Agent, using Fable, a set of hard real world knowledge work problems that deal with enterprise documents. Then score how the agent performs the tasks.

The main differentiators for Fable vs Opus 4.8 is that it doesn't take shortcuts on complex reasoning, it gets multi-step calculations right, and it's significantly more consistent across runs. We saw the biggest leaps in Media & Entertainment (78% vs 61%), Technology (81% vs 73%), Financial Services (89% vs 83%), and Healthcare (66% vs 60%).

Here are some specific examples:

* Legal M&A due diligence: On a task reviewing NDA terms against a semiconductor company's contracting policy, Fable correctly identified that a joint-ownership clause violates exclusivity requirements while a liability cap is permitted under a Super Cap exception. Fable scored 100% vs Opus's 78%.

* Healthcare: On a clinical radiology error audit across 12 reports, Fable precisely categorized each error by severity grade and correctly concluded no Grade 3 errors existed. Opus prematurely escalated a case to "major error requiring immediate departmental review" when the evidence didn't support it — Fable 63% vs Opus 41%.

* Media & Entertainment: On a genre profitability projection task, Fable correctly recognized that a 20% Argentine tax deduction was already embedded in the source spreadsheet figures and didn't double-apply it. Opus applied it again on top — a compounding error across 4 genre calculations that took its score negative on the task vs Fable's 74%.

* Retail analytics: On a task analyzing high-growth product articles against an investment benchmark, Fable correctly computed each article's growth rate individually and identified that only 2 of 5 exceeded the threshold. Opus confused "high growth relative to average" with "above the benchmark" — scoring 61% vs Fable's 94%.

* Financial Services: On a 5-year debt facility projection, Fable correctly applied interest to opening balances and used the right capex figure. Opus applied interest to the total facility amount and computed tax from the wrong base — two compounding errors. Fable scored 83% vs Opus's 62%.

* Technology: On a SaaS feature valuation requiring computation of a Feature Value Index across multiple regions, Fable applied the formula correctly and got exact values for the markets. Opus got the arithmetic wrong on multiple criteria — Fable scored 100% vs Opus's 74%.

Overall, huge step change in complex analysis, work that requires analytical reasoning, and deep domain understanding. Fable will be available shortly in the Box AI Studio for customers to build agents with.
title_cn:
大量证据表明《神鬼寓言》在编码（及相关）任务方面的能力取得了巨大飞跃。这也是复杂知识工作任务的准确性和成功率的重大飞跃。

在我们的 Box AI Complex Work Eval 中，我们针对 Opus 4.8 测试了模型，发现几乎每个行业都有巨大的提升。为了进行评估，我们使用 Fable 为 Box AI 代理提供一组处理企业文档的现实世界知识工作难题。然后对代理执行任务的方式进行评分。

Fable 与 Opus 4.8 的主要区别在于，它不会在复杂推理上走捷径，它能正确进行多步骤计算，并且在运行中显着更加一致。我们看到媒体和娱乐（78% vs 61%）、技术（81% vs 73%）、金融服务（89% vs 83%）和医疗保健（66% vs 60%）方面增幅最大。

以下是一些具体示例：

* 法律并购尽职调查：在根据半导体公司的合同政策审查 NDA 条款的任务中，Fable 正确地识别出共同所有权条款违反了排他性要求，而超级上限例外情况下允许责任上限。 《神鬼寓言》的得分为 100%，而《Opus》的得分为 78%。

* 医疗保健：在对 12 份报告进行的临床放射学错误审核中，Fable 按严重程度对每个错误进行了精确分类，并正确得出不存在 3 级错误的结论。当证据不支持时，Opus 过早地将案件升级为“需要立即进行部门审查的重大错误”——Fable 63% vs Opus 41%。

* 媒体和娱乐：在类型盈利预测任务中，Fable 正确认识到 20% 的阿根廷税收减免已嵌入到源电子表格数据中，并且没有重复应用。 Opus 再次将其应用到了顶部——这是 4 种类型计算中的复合错误，导致该任务的得分为负，而《神鬼寓言》的得分为 74%。

* 零售分析：在一项根据投资基准分析高增长产品文章的任务中，Fable 分别正确计算了每篇文章的增长率，并发现 5 篇文章中只有 2 篇超出了阈值。 Opus 将“相对于平均水平的高增长”与“高于基准”混淆了——得分为 61%，而《寓言》的得分为 94%。

* 金融服务：根据 5 年期债务融资预测，Fable 正确地将利息应用于期初余额，并使用了正确的资本支出数字。 Opus 对贷款总额应用了利息，并根据错误的基数计算了税收——两个复合错误。 《神鬼寓言》的得分为 83%，而《Opus》的得分为 62%。

* 技术：在需要跨多个区域计算功能价值指数的 SaaS 功能评估中，Fable 正确应用了公式并获得了市场的准确值。 Opus 在多项标准上都出现了算术错误——《Fable》得分为 100%，而 Opus 得分为 74%。

总体而言，复杂分析、需要分析推理和深入领域理解的工作发生了巨大的变化。 Fable 很快就会在 Box AI Studio 中提供，供客户用来构建代理。

name: Zara Zhang
title:
People should build agents/skills for their cross-functional teams.

For example, if a design team builds a design agent/skill for the marketing team (trained on all of the brand's guidelines and design patterns), then the marketing team can produce more on-brand assets without having to bug designers every time

But the marketing team couldn't have built this on their own; it takes the designer with their expertise, context, and knowledge

Same thing applies to every pair of teams that work closely with each other, often rely on each other, and complain about each other's limited bandwidth

Building agents for your cross-functional teams ensures each team can be more self-sufficient, and moves us to a direction where teams can be organized by "loops" rather than "functions"
title_cn:
人们应该为他们的跨职能团队建立代理/技能。

例如，如果设计团队为营销团队建立了设计代理/技能（接受了所有品牌指南和设计模式的培训），那么营销团队就可以生产更多的品牌资产，而不必每次都打扰设计师

但营销团队不可能自己建立这个；它需要设计师的专业知识、背景和知识

同样的情况也适用于每一对彼此密切合作、经常相互依赖、并抱怨彼此带宽有限的团队

为跨职能团队构建代理可确保每个团队能够更加自给自足，并使我们朝着可以通过“循环”而不是“功能”组织团队的方向发展

name: Matt Turck
title: 2026 is a BRUTAL grind in VC. You start in Davos, freeze in Aspen, hit Upfront, survive Milken, then it’s straight to Paris for the French Open.  Briefly back in NYC for the Knicks. Then, total blur: SuperReturn in Berlin, Founders Forum in London, then back stateside for the World Cup, back to Paris for Raise AI, Idaho for Sun Valley, quick respite in Mykonos, then the Goldman tech gauntlet, Slush in Finland, NeurIPS in freakin’ Sydney… and *boom*, a productive year of thought leadership and adding value is over, and you’re a wreck.
title_cn: 2026 年对于 VC 来说是残酷的磨练。你从达沃斯出发，在阿斯彭冻结，到达Upfront，在米尔肯幸存下来，然后直接前往巴黎参加法国网球公开赛。  短暂回到纽约为尼克斯队效力。然后，完全模糊：柏林的 SuperReturn，伦敦的创始人论坛，然后回到美国参加世界杯，回到巴黎参加 Raise AI，回到爱达荷州参加 Sun Valley，在米科诺斯岛短暂休息，然后是高盛科技挑战，芬兰的 Slush，悉尼的 NeurIPS……然后*繁荣*，思想领导力和增值的富有成效的一年结束了，你就一团糟了。

name: Peter Yang
title:
Give yourself permission to build.

The traditional career ladder pushes everyone to become a leader, but I just want to be a builder.

As you climb the ladder at most companies, you’re expected to step away from building and fill your time with product reviews, cross-functional alignment, managing up, and performance calibrations.

I know a lot of builders who spent their best years climbing the wrong ladder.

The good news is that this is finally changing. Companies are rewarding builders and ICs more than ever, and even managers are increasingly expected to do IC work too.

But becoming a good builder takes reps, and it’s hard to put in those reps when you’re in back-to-back meetings all day.

So if you’re a builder at heart, embrace it. You don’t have to give up what you’re good at to be a “leader.”

📌 Watch now: https://t.co/wv2SINll18
title_cn:
给自己建造的许可。

传统的职业阶梯推动每个人都成为领导者，但我只想成为一名建设者。

当你在大多数公司晋升时，你需要远离构建，把时间花在产品审查、跨职能协调、向上管理和绩效校准上。

我认识很多建筑商，他们最美好的岁月都爬错了梯子。

好消息是这种情况终于改变了。公司对构建者和 IC 的奖励比以往任何时候都多，甚至管理者也越来越期望从事 IC 工作。

但成为一名优秀的建设者需要重复，而当你整天连续参加会议时，很难投入这些重复。

因此，如果您本质上是一名建设者，请拥抱它。你不必为了成为“领导者”而放弃自己擅长的事情。

📌立即观看：https://t.co/wv2SINll18

name: Madhu Guru
title:
Right from the early days of Gemini, enterprises would get the quality/cost tradeoff wrong. They’d often start with the smallest, cheapest model.

The rule of thumb we gave customers:

Replacing a traditional ML model with an LLM - start small, cos you already know what good looks like.

Building something new - start with the most capable model. Think magically. Figure out what’s actually possible first.

Once they had a high-quality working application, we’d help them move to a smaller model while maintaining quality.
title_cn:
从 Gemini 成立之初起，企业就在质量/成本权衡上犯了错误。他们通常会从最小、最便宜的型号开始。

我们为客户提供的经验法则：

用法学硕士取代传统的机器学习模型——从小事做起，因为你已经知道什么是好的。

构建新事物 - 从最有能力的模型开始。神奇地思考。首先弄清楚什么是实际可能的。

一旦他们拥有了高质量的工作应用程序，我们将帮助他们在保持质量的同时转向更小的模型。

title:
The barrier for non-technical people using coding agents was never the interface. Chatting is the easiest UI ever invented

The barrier is that they don't know what to ask for.

The blank chat box assumes you already know what's possible. Most people don't.

When I tried Town, I was impressed by its onboarding process where the agent proactively suggested workflows & things it can take off my plate, rather than waiting for me to give instructions

障碍在于他们不知道要要求什么。

空白聊天框假定您已经知道可能发生的情况。大多数人都没有。

当我尝试 Town 时，它的入职流程给我留下了深刻的印象，代理主动建议工作流程和可以帮我解决的事情，而不是等待我给出指示

name: Aaron Levie
title:
This is a critical post to read if you’re building an applied AI company right now.

“An application earns its place in the untrainable corner by doing unglamorous work: arranging a company's private reality so a model can act on it, handing the model the tools to act, working with the customer to change the reality of its workforce. A company that brings the translation is tough to copy – and the translation never ends. Integration and maintenance run as long as the relationship does, won by teams that put domain-specialized engineers and tools next to the customer.”

There’s still an insanely large gulf between model capabilities and what it takes to apply them to specific corporate workflows. Some of that is technology that needs to be built, a lot is access to (and formatting of) the right data to work with, and a ton more is on the change management and specific implementation work (FDEs, etc.) it takes to make AI work in any specific corporate setting. 

2 things can be very true at once: frontier models and labs will continue to grow an incredible amount, and there will be a vast ecosystem of software and services companies that emerge to bring the power of these models to real enterprises. This makes room for new infrastructure provides, applied AI companies in every vertical, new versions of system integrators, and more players.

Incredibly exciting time on all fronts.
title_cn:
如果您现在正在建立一家应用人工智能公司，那么这是一篇值得阅读的重要文章。

“应用程序通过做一些乏味的工作而在不可培训的角落赢得一席之地：安排公司的私人现实，以便模型可以对其采取行动，为模型提供行动的工具，与客户合作改变其员工的现实。提供翻译的公司很难复制——而且翻译永远不会结束。只要关系存在，集成和维护就会持续下去，由将领域专业工程师和工具放在客户身边的团队赢得胜利。”

模型功能与将其应用到特定企业工作流程所需的能力之间仍然存在巨大的鸿沟。其中一些是需要构建的技术，很多是访问（和格式化）要使用的正确数据，还有更多是关于使人工智能在任何特定的企业环境中发挥作用所需的变更管理和具体实施工作（FDE 等）。 

有两件事可能同时成为现实：前沿模型和实验室将继续以惊人的数量增长，并且将出现一个庞大的软件和服务公司生态系统，将这些模型的力量带给真正的企业。这为新的基础设施提供者、各个垂直领域的应用人工智能公司、新版本的系统集成商和更多参与者腾出了空间。

各方面都令人难以置信的激动人心的时刻。

name: Peter Yang
title:
Btw this is the prompt I used:

Build F-Zero: futuristic anti-gravity racer with pseudo-3D track (raycasting or mode-7 style scaling), 3 AI opponents, boost meter that drains health, speed 400-800 km/h with visible sense of speed via track warping and scrolling ground pattern, arrow keys to steer, shift to boost, 3 laps on a looping track with checkpoints. 

Style it neon cyberpunk (dark sky, glowing track edges, chromatic aberration on boost), show HUD with position/lap/speed/health, and make the ship bank into turns. Prioritize feel over realism - the speed sensation is the whole game.
title_cn:
顺便说一句，这是我使用的提示：

构建 F-Zero：具有伪 3D 赛道（光线投射或模式 7 风格缩放）的未来反重力赛车，3 个 AI 对手，消耗生命值的助推计，速度 400-800 公里/小时，通过赛道扭曲和滚动地面图案可见速度感，方向键转向，换档加速，在带有检查点的循环赛道上跑 3 圈。

name: Alex Albert
title:
We've reset usage limits across our products! 

For those just starting to test Fable, here's four tips for using it more effectively:
1. Give it bigger, more ambitious tasks than what previous models could handle.
2. Use xhigh/high effort as your default for best performance, med for faster interactive sessions.
3. Rework your skills and CLAUDE.mds. Instructions written for prior models anchor Fable to stale patterns, let it use its own judgment first.
4. Move from providing tasks to providing objectives. Describe what done looks like and how to verify it, then let Fable find the path (/loop and /goal are built for this)
title_cn:
我们重置了产品的使用限制！ 

对于那些刚刚开始测试《神鬼寓言》的人来说，这里有四个更有效地使用它的技巧：
1. 赋予它比以前的模型能够处理的更大、更雄心勃勃的任务。
2. 使用 xhigh/higheffort 作为默认值以获得最佳性能，med 以获得更快的交互会话。
3. 修改你的技能和 CLAUDE.mds。为先前模型编写的指令将 Fable 锚定到过时的模式，让它首先使用自己的判断。
4. 从提供任务转向提供目标。描述完成的内容以及如何验证它，然后让 Fable 找到路径（/loop 和 /goal 为此构建）

name: Boris Cherny
title:
Fable 5 is the biggest step up I’ve felt in our models since Opus 4.5 back in November. After 4.5 came out I uninstalled my IDE when I realized that I’d been doing 100% of my coding in a terminal for a few weeks. With Fable, it’s felt like Claude has stepped up from being a coding agent to a thought and design partner in building the product. Fable has judgement, taste, and dimensionality in a way that previous models didn’t, leading me to trust it more with the most complex work.

I think the first time I had this realization was when I asked Fable to debug something. It is the first model I have used that was so methodical and precise, taking measurements and adding logs then verifying that it truly fixed the issue before declaring victory.

There’s nothing in claude code’s prompting telling the model to do that, it’s just part of its personality. It really has this “big model smell” that I haven’t felt before.
title_cn:
自 11 月份推出 Opus 4.5 以来，《神鬼寓言 5》是我在模型中感受到的最大进步。 4.5 发布后，当我意识到几周以来我 100% 的编码都是在终端中完成时，我卸载了 IDE。在《神鬼寓言》中，克劳德感觉自己已经从一名编码代理晋升为构建产品的思想和设计合作伙伴。 《神鬼寓言》具有以前的模型所没有的判断力、品味和维度，这让我在最复杂的作品上更加信任它。

我想我第一次意识到这一点是当我要求 Fable 调试一些东西时。这是我使用的第一个如此有条理和精确的模型，进行测量并添加日志，然后在宣布胜利之前验证它是否真正解决了问题。

克劳德代码中没有任何提示告诉模型这样做，这只是其个性的一部分。真是有种我从未感受过的“大模味”。

name: Boris Cherny
title:
We talk a lot about how important it is to set up self-verification loops. Especially in the age of powerful models that can run for long periods of time, self-verification is a key ingredient that enables the model to run for much longer, delivering a result that is closer to what you intended, so you can do more without having to constantly check in on Claude as it works.

@delba_oliveira gives a great breakdown of what that looks like and why it matters
title_cn:
我们经常谈论建立自我验证循环的重要性。尤其是在可以长时间运行的强大模型时代，自我验证是使模型能够运行更长时间、提供更接近您预期结果的关键要素，因此您可以做更多事情，而无需不断检查 Claude 的工作情况。

@delba_oliveira 详细介绍了它的外观及其重要性

name: Andrej Karpathy
title:
This is a super exciting release - Claude Fable 5 is the same underlying model as Mythos but with added safeguards. The benchmarks are great and it's SOTA on everything by a margin but I'll add that *qualitatively* also, this is a major-version-bump-deserving step change forward (imo of the same order as Claude 4.5 was in November), peaking especially for long problem-solving sessions on very difficult problems. You can give it a lot more ambitious tasks than what you're used to, the model "gets it" and it will just go, and it's never felt this tempting to stop looking at the code at all (but don't do this in prod!). The model still has quirks that people will run into and the safeguards are configured to be a little too trigger happy for launch, which can hopefully be tuned over time.

I feel a lot of things changing as working software increasingly comes out on a tap. The Jevon's paradox kicks in and I feel my own demand for software growing substantially. You can ask for anything - explainers, visualizers, dashboards, bespoke single-use apps (e.g. a full wandb that is hyper-specific just for your project), you can 10X your test suite, auto-optimize code, run giant research projects with custom HTML for the results, anything! "Free your mind" (Matrix ref). Really looking forward to all the things people build!
title_cn:
这是一个超级令人兴奋的版本 - Claude Fable 5 与 Mythos 具有相同的底层模型，但增加了保护措施。基准测试很棒，而且在所有方面都处于 SOTA 状态，但我还要补充一点，“定性地”，这是一个值得重大版本突破的进步（我认为与 11 月的 Claude 4.5 的顺序相同），尤其是在解决非常困难的问题的长时间问题解决过程中达到顶峰。你可以给它比你习惯的任务更雄心勃勃的任务，模型“明白了”，它就会继续下去，而且从来没有感觉到有这样的诱惑来停止查看代码（但不要在产品中这样做！）。该模型仍然存在人们会遇到的怪癖，并且安全措施的配置有点太不适合启动，希望可以随着时间的推移进行调整。

我觉得随着工作软件越来越多地出现在水龙头上，很多事情都在发生变化。杰文悖论开始出现，我感觉自己对软件的需求大幅增长。您可以要求任何东西 - 解释器、可视化工具、仪表板、定制的一次性应用程序（例如，专门针对您的项目的完整 wandb）、您可以将您的测试套件扩大 10 倍、自动优化代码、使用自定义 HTML 运行大型研究项目以获得结果，等等！ “释放你的思想”（矩阵参考）。真的很期待人们建造的所有东西！

name: Aaron Levie
title:
Great post. So much about model performance is a function of how much compute you’re doing at inference time. This means compute-normalized benchmarks is the only logical path forward. 

And yet, the challenge is it’s a lot harder than it seems given it’s subjective how much compute to apply, which means models behave differently at different thresholds (simplistically, model X’s min thinking may beat model Y’s min thinking, but be reversed at high), and there are a near infinite set of thresholds you could choose to set. 

But either way, moving more in this direction would be great for better understanding AI progress.
title_cn:
很棒的帖子。模型性能在很大程度上取决于您在推理时执行的计算量。这意味着计算标准化基准是唯一合乎逻辑的前进道路。 

然而，挑战在于，考虑到要应用多少计算量是主观的，这比看起来要困难得多，这意味着模型在不同阈值下的表现不同（简单地说，模型 X 的最小思维可能会击败模型 Y 的最小思维，但在高值时会相反），并且您可以选择设置一组近乎无限的阈值。 

但无论哪种方式，朝这个方向发展都将有助于更好地理解人工智能的进展。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
There’s no amount of intelligence that can get packed into AI models that replaces the need for context. For any sufficiently general purpose AI, you will always have to guide it in the direction you want as it has an infinite range of directions it can go in.

As long as the same model is used by a lawyer, an engineer, a financial analyst, or a healthcare professional, and as long as you’re trying to do anything uniquely differentiated or specific, then instructions, domain context, and proprietary data will always need to get into the context window for the model to be useful.

This is partly why AI automation doesn’t come for free, and why there’s still a wide spectrum of who’s getting the largest gains from AI and who’s not. You have to put in real work, and you get real value on the other end.

This is one of the advantages that applied AI will also have in the market. Any layer of abstraction above just the raw intelligence that can meaningfully get you off to the races faster will likely continue to be valuable.
title_cn:
人工智能模型中没有任何智能可以取代对上下文的需求。对于任何足够通用的人工智能，你总是必须引导它朝你想要的方向发展，因为它可以进入的方向范围是无限的。

只要律师、工程师、财务分析师或医疗保健专业人员使用相同的模型，并且只要您尝试做任何独特差异化或特定的事情，那么指令、领域上下文和专有数据将始终需要进入上下文窗口，模型才能发挥作用。

这就是为什么人工智能自动化并非免费的部分原因，也是为什么仍然有很多人从人工智能中获得最大收益而谁却没有。你必须投入真正的工作，然后你才能在另一端获得真正的价值。

这也是应用人工智能在市场上也将具有的优势之一。任何高于原始智能的抽象层都可以有意义地让你更快地进入比赛，这可能会继续有价值。

name: Nikunj Kothari
title:

name: Swyx
title:
It's finally out!!! @METR_Evals found that more than half of SWEBench results is unmergeable slop.  FrontierCode represents over 1000+ hours of maintainer validated software engineering work most frontier models cannot yet solve, much less solve with high quality.

Cog had IOI Gold medalists and top code maintainers Look At The Data — FrontierCode includes 3000+ rubrics covering code quality and anticheat reward hacking plaguing other benchmarks. 

FC Diamond is so hard that Opus 4.8 scores 13.8%.

Three eras of AI coding : Three eras of benchmarks

2021 • Autocomplete : HumanEval
2023 • Passing Tests: SWEBench, TerminalBench
2026 • Maintainable Code: FrontierCode

to me the most beautiful chart when I requested a special historical run into all extant old models, the data was finding that the easiest third of FC tasks (in FC Extended) were rapidlly and suddenly solved over late 2025 - Opus almost doubled from a 41% pass rate to 74% in 4 months. 

This describes the "WTF happened in Dec 2025" vibe shift that a lot of folks from @dhh to @karpathy have called out: it is the difference between getting 95% success in 2 rerolls vs 6, making it finally feasible to go up the next layer of abstraction in agentic coding, eg @GeoffreyHuntley's ralph loops or @bcherny's /goals or  @steipete's "loops that prompt your agents" without fearing too much that things go off the rails.

My guess: as AI accelerates from here, each FrontierCode tier will saturate in sequence, hopefully ~annually. I've already asked the team to prepare FrontierCode 2027....

The old mountains will be destroyed. Their rubble becomes regolith. And from that regolith, the next model forest grows. Circle of life.
title_cn:
终于出来了！！！ @METR_Evals 发现超过一半的 SWEBench 结果是不可合并的溢出。  FrontierCode 代表了超过 1000 多个小时的维护者验证的软件工程工作，大多数前沿模型尚无法解决，更不用说高质量解决了。

Cog 拥有 IOI 金牌获得者和顶级代码维护者 查看数据 — FrontierCode 包含 3000 多个标准，涵盖代码质量和困扰其他基准的反作弊奖励黑客行为。 

FC Diamond 太难了，Opus 4.8 得分为 13.8%。

人工智能编码的三个时代：基准的三个时代

2021 • 自动完成：HumanEval
2023 • 通过测试：SWEBench、TerminalBench
2026 • 可维护的代码：FrontierCode

对我来说，最漂亮的图表是当我要求对所有现存的旧模型进行特殊历史运行时，数据发现最简单的三分之一的 FC 任务（在 FC 扩展中）在 2025 年末迅速突然解决 - Opus 在 4 个月内从 41% 的通过率几乎翻了一番，达到 74%。 

这描述了“WTF 发生在 2025 年 12 月”的氛围转变，许多从 @dhh 到 @karpathy 的人都指出了这一点：这是 2 次重新滚动与 6 次重新滚动获得 95% 成功率之间的区别，使得最终可以在代理编码中进入下一个抽象层，例如 @GeoffreyHuntley 的 ralph 循环或 @bcherny 的 /goals 或 @steipete 的“提示你的代理的循环”，而无需太担心事情会偏离轨道。

我的猜测：随着人工智能从这里开始加速，每个 FrontierCode 层将按顺序饱和，希望每年饱和。我已经要求团队准备 FrontierCode 2027....

旧山将被毁。他们的瓦砾变成了风化层。下一个模型森林就是从风化层中生长出来的。生活圈子。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
The numbers may be a bit extreme here, but unquestionably use-cases have to stratify in the next year or two between model families.

We’ll see a split between frontier intelligence for high end tasks and work, and much cheaper models for high volume workloads that can sufficiently be peeled off to cheaper models. Frontier will still be far bigger than today because the use-cases will demand it, but the low-end will get quite a bit larger as well.

The big update here is that the layer that can efficiently route the workload to the right model will then become increasingly valuable since that becomes one of the new hard problems in AI agents. Agent orchestration that can cost optimize while still performing the task successfully will be in a strong position.
title_cn:
这里的数字可能有点极端，但毫无疑问，用例必须在未来一两年内在模型系列之间进行分层。

我们将看到用于高端任务和工作的前沿智能与用于大容量工作负载的更便宜的模型之间的分裂，这些模型可以充分剥离到更便宜的模型。 Frontier 仍将比今天大得多，因为用例会需要它，但低端也会变得更大。

这里的重大更新是，能够有效地将工作负载路由到正确模型的层将变得越来越有价值，因为这成为人工智能代理中的新难题之一。可以优化成本同时仍然成功执行任务的代理编排将处于有利地位。

name: Boris Cherny
title:
Seeing a number of benchmarks showing Opus is the best model for long-running work.

Five tips for running Opus autonomously for hours/days:

1. Use auto mode for permissions, so Claude doesn’t ask for approval
2. Use dynamic workflows, to have Claude orchestrate hundreds/thousands of agents to get a task done
3. Use /goal or /loop, to nudge Claude to keep going until it’s done
4. Use Claude Code in the cloud, so you can close your laptop (easiest way is the desktop or mobile app)
5. Make sure Claude has a way to self-verify its work end to end: Claude in Chrome browser extension for web, iOS/Android sim MCP for mobile, a way to start the full web server or service for backend work
title_cn:
许多基准测试表明 Opus 是长期运行工作的最佳模型。

自主运行 Opus 数小时/数天的五个技巧：

1.使用自动模式获取权限，这样克劳德就不会请求批准
2. 使用动态工作流程，让 Claude 协调数百/数千个代理来完成任务
3. 使用 /goal 或 /loop 来推动 Claude 继续前进，直到完成
4.在云端使用Claude Code，这样你就可以关闭你的笔记本电脑（最简单的方法是桌面或移动应用程序）
5. 确保 Claude 有办法端到端地自我验证其工作：Claude 在 Chrome 浏览器扩展中用于 Web、iOS/Android sim MCP 用于移动设备、启动完整 Web 服务器或后端工作服务的方法

name: Aaron Levie
title:
This is what the market got wrong about AI eating enterprise software. Building good software in the past was very hard. Yes, AI has made that a bit easier, though it’s still hard to build something that’s got good taste, differentiated, high quality, secure, and so on.

But nevertheless, that’s only one component of building a platform that enterprises rely on. The plurality of costs in most enterprise software companies is actually on GTM, because at scale most enterprise software categories are tough to break into and need a heavy amount of consultative selling and support for implementation and integration of solutions.  

AI hasn’t reduced the need for that, and in many cases requires it even more now, as landscapes get even more busy and complicated for buyers to navigate through. If you make one thing cheaper and more abundant (development of software) then the new problem of discoverability and market differentiation (GTM) becomes the hardest part.
title_cn:
这就是市场对人工智能吞噬企业软件的错误认识。过去构建优秀的软件非常困难。是的，人工智能让这一切变得更加容易，尽管构建具有良好品味、差异化、高质量、安全等的东西仍然很困难。

但尽管如此，这只是构建企业依赖的平台的组成部分之一。大多数企业软件公司的多元化成本实际上都在GTM上，因为从规模上看，大多数企业软件类别很难进入，需要大量的咨询销售以及解决方案实施和集成的支持。  

人工智能并没有减少这种需求，而且在许多情况下，现在甚至更需要它，因为买家的导航变得更加繁忙和复杂。如果你让一件东西变得更便宜、更丰富（软件开发），那么可发现性和市场差异化（GTM）的新问题就成为最难的部分。

name: Aditya Agarwal
title:
I have had the chance to go through two IPOs (Meta and Dropbox).

Fabulous wealth tends to amplify deeper desires, not create new ones.

The mainstream narrative is: "Early Employees make lots of money and buy mansions and go to the beach".

For some: Yes, they want to chill. That is great. I am happy for them.

But for a lot of folks, it is a chance to do even crazier and wackier stuff. Start new things. Fund new things. Keep the crazy loop of Silicon Valley going.

It's going to be awesome to have a lot of folks with liquidity in the coming months. 

Onwards!.
title_cn:
我有机会经历过两次 IPO（Meta 和 Dropbox）。

巨额财富往往会放大更深层次的欲望，而不是创造新的欲望。

主流的说法是：“早期员工赚很多钱，买豪宅，去海滩”。

对于一些人来说：是的，他们想冷静一下。那太好了。我为他们感到高兴。

但对于很多人来说，这是一个做更疯狂、更古怪事情的机会。开始新的事情。资助新事物。让硅谷的疯狂循环继续下去。

在接下来的几个月里，如果有很多人拥有流动性，那就太棒了。 

继续！

name: Madhu Guru
title:
A common misconception is that training data is low skill, grunt work - scan some notebooks, mine the internet, create labeled samples.

The data required to advance the model frontier is the opposite. Labs need training data for high-economic-value tasks. And most of these tasks outside of SWE have little documentation - it is complex, domain-specific knowledge built over the  years, spanning legacy tools that don’t talk to each other.

That's why we have SWE agents and not knowledge work agents yet.

The companies creating this training data, such as Mercor, are doing extremely high-leverage, high-skill work. 

Critical to moving AI forward. And deeply underappreciated.
title_cn:
一个常见的误解是训练数据是低技能、繁重的工作——扫描一些笔记本、挖掘互联网、创建标记样本。

推进模型前沿所需的数据则相反。实验室需要训练数据来完成高经济价值的任务。 SWE 之外的大多数任务都没有什么文档——它们是多年来构建的复杂的、特定领域的知识，涵盖了彼此不交互的遗留工具。

这就是为什么我们还没有 SWE 代理而不是知识工作代理。

创建此类培训数据的公司（例如 Mercor）正在进行极高杠杆率、高技能的工作。 

对于推动人工智能向前发展至关重要。并且被深深地低估了。

name: Swyx
title:
one popular theory is that research paper alpha* and lab publishing ~died when researchers realized that instead of fighting with marketing depts they could simply walk out the door and get >$100m for their legally protected tacit knowledge gained

california non-noncompetes have a bigger impact on knowledge spreading than github, arxiv, and huggingface combined

*btw this is a motivator for me to set up @aidotengineer as a product-centric industry conference to complement the paper-centric research conferences
title_cn:
一种流行的理论是，研究论文 alpha* 和实验室出版 ~ 消亡，因为研究人员意识到，他们不必与营销部门抗争，只需走出门，就可以获得超过 1 亿美元的受法律保护的隐性知识

加州非竞争性组织对知识传播的影响比 github、arxiv 和 Huggingface 的总和还要大

*顺便说一句，这是我建立@aidotengineer 作为以产品为中心的行业会议的动力，以补充以论文为中心的研究会议

name: Madhu Guru
title:
Routing to models is genuinely hard. It means mapping each task to the right model - which requires benchmarking models against your product's specific tasks and dialing in the quality/cost trade-off. 

And there is an opportunity in that difficulty.

Here is the progression I saw with enterprises while on Gemini.

Phase 1 (2024): Default to the "it" model. Everybody used GPT regardless of task, because it was the shiny new thing.

Phase 2 (early 2025): Over-optimize. Teams over-corrected, looking for the smallest/cheapest model for their task, but did not have evals sophisticated enough to map tasks to models. They ended up burning cycles and shipping slower.

Phase 3: Nuanced routing. The industry’s eval muscle and model diversity got to a point where the most sophisticated AI-native startups succeeded in breaking their product into sub-agents and routed each task to the right model - e.g. hardest reasoning to Claude, simplest to Gemini Flash-Lite or open-weight models.

And like most product patterns, enterprises followed the AI-native builders 6-9 months later.
title_cn:
路由到模型确实很困难。这意味着将每个任务映射到正确的模型 - 这需要根据产品的特定任务对模型进行基准测试，并进行质量/成本权衡。 

在这种困难中也蕴藏着机会。

这是我在双子座上看到的企业进展。

第一阶段（2024 年）：默认为“it”模型。无论什么任务，每个人都使用 GPT，因为它是闪亮的新事物。

第 2 阶段（2025 年初）：过度优化。团队过度纠正，为他们的任务寻找最小/最便宜的模型，但没有足够复杂的评估来将任务映射到模型。他们最终导致了燃烧周期和运输速度变慢。

第三阶段：细致入微的路由。该行业的评估能力和模型多样性已经达到了这样的程度：最先进的人工智能原生初创公司成功地将其产品分解为子代理，并将每个任务路由到正确的模型 - 例如对 Claude 来说最难的推理，对 Gemini Flash-Lite 或开放重量模型来说最简单。

与大多数产品模式一样，企业在 6-9 个月后追随了 AI 原生构建者。

name: Aaron Levie
title:
Token costs are becoming one of the hottest topics for any enterprise I talk with right now. It’s very bullish for AI in general because it means these systems are being used at a scale that wasn’t contemplated before. 

It also gives way to another form of differentiation that will emerge for the applied AI layer, which is model routing.

As tokens take on a significant amount of the cost of any given workflow, then companies will inevitably want to ensure that their dollars go into the most efficient use of tokens for the particular job at hand. 

Frontier intelligence will always be relevant at the high end of tasks, like coding, legal and financial analysis, healthcare, and more. And dollars spent here will only go up over time. But, equally, you can peel off individual tasks to lower cost models (whether they’re from open weights vendors or the major labs) and deliver a more efficient end outcome. 

To do this effectively, the applied AI layer needs to understand the workflows in their domain better than anyone else, and be able to mix and match models to different jobs. If you’re doing document extraction, you need to know which models perform better or worse for any given document type. If you’re legal analysis, you want to know which models perform various types of tasks best. And so on.

This will become one of the bigger differentiation points over time. The companies with the best evals, the best ability to route the workloads, and those that have business models directly aligned to customers financial goals, will be in a great position.
title_cn:
对于我现在交谈的任何企业来说，代币成本正在成为最热门的话题之一。总体而言，这对人工智能非常乐观，因为这意味着这些系统的使用规模是以前没有想到的。 

它还让位于应用人工智能层将出现的另一种差异化形式，即模型路由。

由于代币在任何给定工作流程中占据大量成本，因此公司将不可避免地希望确保他们的资金用于最有效地使用代币来完成手头的特定工作。 

前沿情报始终与高端任务相关，例如编码、法律和财务分析、医疗保健等。随着时间的推移，花在这里的钱只会增加。但同样，您可以将单个任务剥离为成本更低的模型（无论它们来自开放权重供应商还是主要实验室），并提供更高效的最终结果。 

为了有效地做到这一点，应用人工智能层需要比其他人更好地理解其领域的工作流程，并能够将模型混合和匹配到不同的工作。如果您正在进行文档提取，您需要知道哪些模型对于任何给定的文档类型表现更好或更差。如果您从事法律分析，您想知道哪些模型最能执行各种类型的任务。等等。

随着时间的推移，这将成为更大的差异点之一。拥有最佳评估、最有能力安排工作负载的公司，以及那些拥有与客户财务目标直接一致的业务模式的公司，将处于有利地位。

name: Swyx
title:
a smarter alternative to "always use plan mode": 

always frame your task as a question, so that the model is invited to push back and rate the quality of the idea/suggest alternatives, rather than blindly execute what you SAID to do 

(which is often not precisely what you MEANT)

literally just appending "?" to the end of your prompt often does it
title_cn:
“始终使用计划模式”的更智能替代方案： 

始终将您的任务视为一个问题，以便模型被邀请推翻并评估想法/建议替代方案的质量，而不是盲目执行您所说的事情 

（这通常不完全是你的意思）

从字面上看只是附加“？”到提示结束时通常会这样做

name: Aaron Levie
title:
Coding is basically the pinnacle of what you could reasonably automate with AI, and yet we still need human engineers to oversee agents for them to be effective.

The AI models are trained on an incredible amount of sophisticated code. The users are highly technical and can use the latest tools quickly. The work is “verifiable” because you can test an app. The outcomes are often removed from the quality of the code (you can have sloppy code but the app can still work). And the context for the agent is often already digitized and sitting in the codebase. 

That’s an incredible amount of benefits that AI coding agents get to work with. Some of those apply to knowledge work, but most don’t in areas where the work needs to be fully reviewed to be useful, or where data isn’t as abundantly digitized. This makes the job for agents in knowledge work more complicated.

So if with all of that, engineers still remain in very high demand, the risks are going to be less than what’s perceived for other areas of knowledge work. Agents will let people do far more than they did before, but the people don’t go away.
title_cn:
编码基本上是人工智能合理自动化的顶峰，但我们仍然需要人类工程师来监督代理以使其有效。

人工智能模型接受了大量复杂代码的训练。用户技术含量高，可以快速使用最新的工具。该工作是“可验证的”，因为您可以测试应用程序。结果通常与代码的质量无关（您可能有草率的代码，但应用程序仍然可以运行）。代理的上下文通常已经数字化并位于代码库中。 

人工智能编码代理可以享受到令人难以置信的好处。其中一些适用于知识工作，但大多数不适用于需要充分审查工作才能发挥作用的领域，或者数据没有充分数字化的领域。这使得知识工作代理人的工作变得更加复杂。

因此，如果尽管如此，工程师的需求仍然非常高，那么风险将低于其他知识工作领域的风险。特工会让人们做比以前更多的事情，但人们不会离开。

name: Madhu Guru
title:
One of the most common mistakes I see enterprise AI teams make is building for today’s model capabilities and price points.

Think 6 months out. Models will be way smarter and cheaper.

Scaffold around today’s model weaknesses to push the frontier. Bet that the next generation of models will natively solve for the scaffold. Push the frontier again.

Build for the slope.

Over time, that ability to repeatedly identify and bridge model gaps becomes a moat of its own.
title_cn:
我看到企业人工智能团队最常犯的错误之一是针对当今的模型功能和价格点进行构建。

想想6个月后。模型将变得更加智能、更加便宜。

围绕当今模型的弱点搭建支架，推动前沿发展。打赌下一代模型将原生解决脚手架问题。再次突破边界。

为斜坡建造。

随着时间的推移，这种反复识别和弥合模型差距的能力就会成为自己的护城河。

name: Peter Yang
title:
How to build AI skills that check their own work and improve over time:

1. Give it context

Ask AI: "Create a skill for this [repeated task]. Here are examples of good output so it knows what good looks like."

2. Make it easy to trigger

“Write a clear skill description using this pattern: Use when the user wants to [do this].”

3. Add evals

"Create an evals md with 10 pass/fail checks for common errors in the skill's output."

4. Add memory

"Create a memory md to capture one-sentence learnings from past chats using this skill."

5. Build a skill to edit skills

"Create a skill that cleans up other skills by removing duplicate or stale instructions, vague rules, and AI slop."

📌 Watch my full walkthrough here: https://t.co/u434ytNSZd
title_cn:
如何培养人工智能技能来检查自己的工作并随着时间的推移而提高：

1. 给出背景

询问人工智能：“为此[重复任务]创建一项技能。以下是良好输出的示例，因此它知道什么是良好的。”

2、让触发变得容易

“使用此模式编写清晰的技能描述：当用户想要[执行此操作]时使用。”

3.添加评估

“创建一个 evals md，对技能输出中的常见错误进行 10 次通过/失败检查。”

4.添加内存

“使用此技能创建一个记忆 MD，以捕获从过去的聊天中学到的一句话。”

5.建立编辑技能的技能

“创建一项技能，通过删除重复或陈旧的指令、模糊的规则和人工智能废话来清理其他技能。”

📌 在这里观看我的完整演练：https://t.co/u434ytNSZd

name: Peter Yang
title:
I spent the whole day today setting up integrations and skills in Codex for my top creator workflows.

Now I'm convinced that you can save at least 50% of your time on any type of knowledge work if you just set up the system upfront.

Note that all my workflows have human checkpoints along the way so I can apply my "taste."

Anyway, it feels extremely liberating to have all this set up. If you want to do the same, just follow these three steps:

1. Reflect on your past week:
- What work did you spend the most time on?
- What work was the most repetitive?

Pick your most painful, manual workflow to start.

2. List out every single step of that manual workflow. Be very detail oriented.

3. Open Codex (or Claude Code) and paste the list of steps from 2 and ask it "What integrations and skills can I build to streamline this with your help?"

AI will guide you the rest of the way.
title_cn:
今天我花了一整天的时间在 Codex 中为我的顶级创作者工作流程设置集成和技能。

现在我确信，如果您只需预先设置系统，您可以在任何类型的知识工作上节省至少 50% 的时间。

请注意，我的所有工作流程都有人工检查点，因此我可以应用我的“品味”。

不管怎样，完成这一切后感觉非常轻松。如果您想执行相同的操作，只需执行以下三个步骤：

1. 回顾一下你过去的一周：
- 你在哪项工作上花费最多的时间？
- 哪些工作重复率最高？

选择您最痛苦的手动工作流程开始。

2. 列出手动工作流程的每一个步骤。非常注重细节。

3. 打开 Codex（或 Claude Code）并粘贴 2 中的步骤列表并询问“在您的帮助下我可以构建哪些集成和技能来简化此操作？”

人工智能将指导您完成剩下的工作。
